	type AgentLoop struct {
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

<a name="NewAgentLoop"></a>
### func NewAgentLoop

	func NewAgentLoop(opts ...LoopOption) *AgentLoop

NewAgentLoop creates a new agent loop.

<a name="AgentLoop.ClearConversation"></a>
### func \(\*AgentLoop\) ClearConversation

	func (l *AgentLoop) ClearConversation(id string)

ClearConversation removes a conversation.

<a name="AgentLoop.FirewallStats"></a>
### func \(\*AgentLoop\) FirewallStats

	func (l *AgentLoop) FirewallStats() map[string]any

FirewallStats returns a map snapshot of the context firewall counters. Returns nil if the context firewall is not enabled on this agent loop.

<a name="AgentLoop.GetConfig"></a>
### func \(\*AgentLoop\) GetConfig

	func (l *AgentLoop) GetConfig() AgentConfig

GetConfig returns the current configuration.

<a name="AgentLoop.GetConversation"></a>
### func \(\*AgentLoop\) GetConversation

	func (l *AgentLoop) GetConversation(id string) *Conversation

GetConversation returns a conversation by ID.

<a name="AgentLoop.HandleMessage"></a>
### func \(\*AgentLoop\) HandleMessage

	func (l *AgentLoop) HandleMessage(ctx context.Context, message string) (string, error)

HandleMessage processes a single message without conversation context.

<a name="AgentLoop.Run"></a>
### func \(\*AgentLoop\) Run

	func (l *AgentLoop) Run(ctx context.Context, messages <-chan *AgentMessage, responses chan<- *AgentResponse) error

Run starts the agent loop in a continuous mode, processing messages from a channel. This is useful for daemon mode where messages arrive asynchronously.

<a name="AgentLoop.RunOnce"></a>
### func \(\*AgentLoop\) RunOnce

	func (l *AgentLoop) RunOnce(ctx context.Context, userMessage, conversationID string) (response string, err error)

RunOnce processes a single user turn through the full reasoning loop.

<a name="AgentLoop.RunWithSkill"></a>
### func \(\*AgentLoop\) RunWithSkill

	func (l *AgentLoop) RunWithSkill(ctx context.Context, skill *skills.Skill, input string, conversationID string) (string, error)

RunWithSkill executes a skill through the agent loop with the skill's constraints applied \(tool filtering, iteration limits\). The skill body is injected as the system prompt, and if the skill declares allowed\-tools, the tool registry is filtered to only include those tools for the duration of execution.

<a name="AgentLoop.RunWithTask"></a>
### func \(\*AgentLoop\) RunWithTask

	func (l *AgentLoop) RunWithTask(ctx context.Context, t *task.Task) (string, error)

RunWithTask processes a task through the agent loop with memory context injection.

<a name="AgentLoop.SetBranchManager"></a>
### func \(\*AgentLoop\) SetBranchManager

	func (l *AgentLoop) SetBranchManager(mgr any)

SetBranchManager wires a branch manager for in\-memory cache coordination. Stub implementation: the branch manager reference is retained for future use.

<a name="AgentLoop.SetCapabilityIndex"></a>
### func \(\*AgentLoop\) SetCapabilityIndex

	func (l *AgentLoop) SetCapabilityIndex(ci *skills.CapabilityIndex)

SetCapabilityIndex sets the capability index for skill discovery. This allows wiring the index after the loop is created when skills are initialized in a specific order.

<a name="AgentLoop.SetConfig"></a>
### func \(\*AgentLoop\) SetConfig

	func (l *AgentLoop) SetConfig(config AgentConfig)

SetConfig updates the agent configuration.

<a name="AgentLoop.SetContextFirewallConfig"></a>

	func (l *AgentLoop) SetContextFirewallConfig(fw config.LLMContextFirewallConfig)

SetContextFirewallConfig wires context firewall settings from the user\-facing config schema into the agent loop config.

<a name="AgentLoop.SetMCPServerLister"></a>
### func \(\*AgentLoop\) SetMCPServerLister

	func (l *AgentLoop) SetMCPServerLister(lister func() []MCPServerInfo)

SetMCPServerLister wires a callback that returns current MCP server info. Used by the system prompt builder to include available MCP tools.

<a name="AgentLoop.SetMemvidClient"></a>
### func \(\*AgentLoop\) SetMemvidClient

	func (l *AgentLoop) SetMemvidClient(client *memvid.Client)

SetMemvidClient sets the memvid client after construction. This allows wiring the client after the loop is created when dependencies are initialized in a specific order.

<a name="AgentLoop.SetPrefetchCallback"></a>

	func (l *AgentLoop) SetPrefetchCallback(callback func(query string, maxItems int))

<a name="AgentLoop.SetSessionStore"></a>

	func (l *AgentLoop) SetSessionStore(store any, sessionCfg any)

SetSessionStore wires a session store and config for persistence. Stub implementation: the session store reference is retained for future use.

	func (l *AgentLoop) SetSkillLoader(loader *skills.LazySkillLoader)

<a name="AgentLoop.SetTaskStore"></a>
### func \(\*AgentLoop\) SetTaskStore

	func (l *AgentLoop) SetTaskStore(store *task.Store)

SetTaskStore sets the task store after construction. This allows wiring the store after the loop is created when dependencies are initialized in a specific order.

<a name="AgentMemoryConfig"></a>
## type AgentMemoryConfig

AgentMemoryConfig holds memory recall configuration for an agent.

	type AgentMemoryConfig struct {
	    RecallMode MemoryRecallMode `json:"recall_mode"`
	    // SnapshotCachingEnabled controls whether memory snapshots are frozen for
	    // LLM prefix caching (Hermes pattern). When false, FreezeMemorySnapshot is
	    // skipped and the live context is used each turn.
	    SnapshotCachingEnabled bool `json:"snapshot_caching_enabled"`
	}

<a name="AgentReport"></a>
## type AgentReport

AgentReport represents the structured report from an agent's response.

	type AgentReport struct {
	    // Status indicates the completion state: completed, partial, failed, needs_input
	    Status string `json:"status"`
	
	    // Accomplished lists what the agent completed.
	    Accomplished []string `json:"accomplished"`
	
	    // NotDone lists what was not completed.
	    NotDone []string `json:"not_done,omitempty"`
	
	    // Issues lists problems encountered.
	    Issues []string `json:"issues,omitempty"`
	
	    // Observations provides context for follow-up work.
	    Observations []string `json:"observations,omitempty"`
	
	    // SuggestedNextAgent is the ID of the agent that should handle follow-up.
	    SuggestedNextAgent string `json:"suggested_next_agent,omitempty"`
	
	    // UserDecisionNeeded indicates if user input is required to proceed.
	    UserDecisionNeeded bool `json:"user_decision_needed"`
	
	    // DecisionContext explains what decision the user needs to make.
	    DecisionContext string `json:"decision_context,omitempty"`
	}

<a name="ExtractReport"></a>
### func ExtractReport

	func ExtractReport(response string) *AgentReport

ExtractReport parses an AgentReport from the agent's response. It looks for a JSON code block containing a status field. Returns nil if no valid report is found.

<a name="AgentReport.HasIssues"></a>
### func \(\*AgentReport\) HasIssues

	func (r *AgentReport) HasIssues() bool

HasIssues returns true if there were any issues reported.

<a name="AgentReport.IsComplete"></a>
### func \(\*AgentReport\) IsComplete

	func (r *AgentReport) IsComplete() bool

IsComplete returns true if the status is completed.

<a name="AgentReport.NeedsRouting"></a>
### func \(\*AgentReport\) NeedsRouting

	func (r *AgentReport) NeedsRouting() bool

NeedsRouting returns true if the report suggests routing to another agent.

<a name="AgentReport.NeedsUserInput"></a>
### func \(\*AgentReport\) NeedsUserInput

	func (r *AgentReport) NeedsUserInput() bool

NeedsUserInput returns true if user decision is required.

<a name="AgentResponse"></a>

AgentResponse represents the agent's response.

	type AgentResponse struct {
	    ConversationID string `json:"conversation_id"`
	    Content        string `json:"content"`
	    Error          error  `json:"error,omitempty"`
	    ReplyTo        string `json:"reply_to,omitempty"`
	}

<a name="AgentRole"></a>
## type AgentRole

AgentRole defines the role an agent plays in the system.

	type AgentRole string

<a name="RoleDispatcher"></a>

	const (
	    // RoleDispatcher is the intake agent that classifies and routes requests.
	    RoleDispatcher AgentRole = "dispatcher"
	    // RoleExecutor is a specialist agent that executes specific types of tasks.
	    RoleExecutor AgentRole = "executor"
	    // RoleReviewer is an agent that reviews and validates work.
	    RoleReviewer AgentRole = "reviewer"
	    // RoleBot is a persistent autonomous agent that runs on triggers.
	    RoleBot AgentRole = "bot"
	)

<a name="AgentSpec"></a>
## type AgentSpec

AgentSpec defines the specification for creating an agent.

	type AgentSpec struct {
	    // ID is the unique identifier for this agent specification.
	    ID  string `json:"id"`
	    // Name is a human-readable name for the agent.
	    Name string `json:"name"`
	    // Role defines the agent's role in the system.
	    Role AgentRole `json:"role"`
	    // Purpose is a description of what this agent does (used in system prompt).
	    Purpose string `json:"purpose"`
	    // Model can be an alias name (e.g., "coder"), a direct model reference (e.g., "zai/glm-4.7"),
	    // or empty to use the default. If it matches a known alias, alias resolution with
	    // automatic failover and cooldown rotation is used.
	    Model string `json:"model,omitempty"`
	    // AdditionalTools are tools beyond the baseline that this agent has access to.
	    AdditionalTools []string `json:"additional_tools,omitempty"`
	    // Constraints are operational limits for this agent.
	    Constraints AgentConstraints `json:"constraints"`
	    // SystemPromptSections are additional prompt sections for this agent.
	    SystemPromptSections []string `json:"system_prompt_sections,omitempty"`
	    // AvailableSkills lists skill names this agent can invoke.
	    AvailableSkills []string `json:"available_skills,omitempty"`
	    // SkillTriggers maps keywords to skill names for automatic invocation.
	    SkillTriggers map[string]string `json:"skill_triggers,omitempty"`
	}

<a name="AggregatedTaskReport"></a>
## type AggregatedTaskReport

AggregatedTaskReport represents the final report for a completed task.

	type AggregatedTaskReport struct {
	    // Summary is a brief overview of what was accomplished
	    Summary string `json:"summary"`
	    // StepsCompleted is the number of steps that completed successfully
	    StepsCompleted int `json:"steps_completed"`
	    // StepsTotal is the total number of steps
	    StepsTotal int `json:"steps_total"`
	    // Recommendations are categorized recommendations from all agents
	    Recommendations []CategorizedRecommendation `json:"recommendations"`
	    // ExecutionTime is the total execution time
	    ExecutionTime string `json:"execution_time"`
	}

	type AmendmentSubmitter interface {
	    Submit(ctx context.Context, req *task.AmendmentRequest) error
	    Process(ctx context.Context, requestID string) (*task.AmendmentReply, error)
	}

<a name="ArtifactManager"></a>
## type ArtifactManager

ArtifactManager integrates Claude artifact detection and context building with the agent loop. It automatically detects and utilizes CLAUDE.md and .claude/ artifacts to enhance agent prompts with project\-specific context.

	type ArtifactManager struct {
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

	func (am *ArtifactManager) BuildArtifactContextSection(taskDesc, workingDir string) string

BuildArtifactContextSection builds a context section for inclusion in system prompts. This is the main integration point with the agent loop.

	func (am *ArtifactManager) BuildCommandSuggestions(taskDesc, workingDir string) string

<a name="ArtifactManager.BuildFullArtifactContext"></a>
### func \(\*ArtifactManager\) BuildFullArtifactContext

	func (am *ArtifactManager) BuildFullArtifactContext(taskDesc, workingDir string) string

BuildFullArtifactContext builds a comprehensive context section combining project context, commands, skills, and agents.

CLAUDE.md content is included verbatim \(not lossy\-summarized\) because it contains authoritative project instructions. README.md content is included as a summary since READMEs can be very large.

<a name="ArtifactManager.BuildSkillReferences"></a>
### func \(\*ArtifactManager\) BuildSkillReferences

	func (am *ArtifactManager) BuildSkillReferences(taskDesc, workingDir string) string

BuildSkillReferences builds a section with relevant skill references.

FindAgentForTask finds a relevant agent from .claude/agents/ for a task.

	func (am *ArtifactManager) FindSkillForTask(taskDesc, workingDir string) *artifactcontext.Skill

FindSkillForTask finds a relevant skill from .claude/skills/ for a task.

<a name="ArtifactManager.LoadAgentsContext"></a>
### func \(\*ArtifactManager\) LoadAgentsContext

	func (am *ArtifactManager) LoadAgentsContext(filePath string) string

LoadAgentsContext loads hierarchical AGENTS.md files relative to filePath and returns them joined as a single string suitable for prompt injection. If projectRoot is not set, or no AGENTS.md files are found, returns "". The total output is capped at maxAgentsContextBytes.

<a name="ArtifactManager.ScanDirectory"></a>
### func \(\*ArtifactManager\) ScanDirectory

	func (am *ArtifactManager) ScanDirectory(dir string) (*artifactcontext.Artifacts, error)

ScanDirectory scans a directory for Claude artifacts and caches the results.

<a name="ArtifactManager.WithProjectRoot"></a>
### func \(\*ArtifactManager\) WithProjectRoot

	func (am *ArtifactManager) WithProjectRoot(root string) *ArtifactManager

WithProjectRoot sets the project root directory for hierarchical AGENTS.md loading. This must be called before LoadAgentsContext to have effect.

<a name="Attempt"></a>
## type Attempt

Attempt represents a single actor\-\>reviewer round within a pair session.

	type Attempt struct {
	    // Round is the 1-indexed attempt number.
	    Round int `json:"round"`
	
	    // ActorOutput is the raw output from the actor agent.
	    ActorOutput string `json:"actor_output"`
	
	    // ActorStepID is the step ID that produced this attempt's actor output.
	    ActorStepID string `json:"actor_step_id"`
	
	    // Review is the reviewer's verdict for this attempt.
	    Review *ReviewResult `json:"review,omitempty"`
	
	    // ReviewerStepID is the step ID that produced this attempt's review.
	    ReviewerStepID string `json:"reviewer_step_id,omitempty"`
	
	    // StartedAt is when this attempt began.
	    StartedAt time.Time `json:"started_at"`
	
	    // CompletedAt is when the review came back.
	    CompletedAt time.Time `json:"completed_at,omitempty"`
	}

<a name="Attempt.Satisfied"></a>
### func \(\*Attempt\) Satisfied

	func (a *Attempt) Satisfied() bool

Satisfied returns true if this attempt's review approved the work.

<a name="BeforeProviderPayloadData"></a>

	type BeforeProviderPayloadData struct {
	    ModelID  string `json:"model_id"`
	    Payload  string `json:"payload"`
	    Endpoint string `json:"endpoint"`
	}

	    ModelID    string               `json:"model_id"`
	    Messages   []llm.ChatMessage    `json:"messages"`
	    Tools      []llm.ToolDefinition `json:"tools,omitempty"`
	    TokenCount int                  `json:"token_count"`
	}

<a name="BeforeToolCallHook"></a>
## type BeforeToolCallHook

BeforeToolCallHook is called before a tool is executed. Return BlockResult with Block=true to prevent execution.

	type BeforeToolCallHook interface {
	    BeforeToolCall(ctx context.Context, toolCall llm.ToolCall) BlockResult
	}

<a name="BlockResult"></a>
## type BlockResult

BlockResult is returned by BeforeToolCallHook.

	type BlockResult struct {
	    Block  bool
	    Reason string // human-readable reason if blocked
	}

<a name="BusPairSessionState"></a>
## type BusPairSessionState

BusPairSessionState represents the current state of a bus\-channel pair session.

	type BusPairSessionState struct {
	    SessionID     string      `json:"session_id"`
	    ActorID       string      `json:"actor_id"`
	    ReviewerID    string      `json:"reviewer_id"`
	    CurrentTurn   int         `json:"current_turn"`
	    MaxTurns      int         `json:"max_turns"`
	    Phase         string      `json:"phase"` // "actor_turn", "reviewer_turn", "completed", "failed"
	    LastVerdict   PairVerdict `json:"last_verdict,omitempty"`
	    Turns         []PairTurn  `json:"turns,omitempty"`
	    InitialPrompt string      `json:"initial_prompt"`
	}

<a name="CacheConfig"></a>
## type CacheConfig

CacheConfig holds configuration for the result cache.

	type CacheConfig struct {
	    // MaxEntries is the maximum number of entries to keep in the cache.
	    MaxEntries int
	    // DefaultTTL is the default time-to-live for cache entries.
	    DefaultTTL time.Duration
	    // CleanupFreq is the frequency of background cleanup.
	    CleanupFreq time.Duration
	    // EnabledTools is the list of tools that are cacheable.
	    EnabledTools []string
	}

<a name="ChatDelegation"></a>
## type ChatDelegation

ChatDelegation is a simple delegation from the chat agent. This is NOT in JSONL format \- it's a simpler struct for chat\-to\-dispatcher handoff.

	type ChatDelegation struct {
	    // Intent is what the user wants to accomplish.
	    Intent string `json:"intent"`
	
	    // Urgency is the conversational assessment of urgency.
	    Urgency string `json:"urgency"`
	
	    // Context is relevant conversation context.
	    Context string `json:"context"`
	
	    // OriginalMessage is the user's original request.
	    OriginalMessage string `json:"original_message"`
	}

<a name="CollaborationEngine"></a>
## type CollaborationEngine

CollaborationEngine manages collaboration sessions and registered modes.

Each mode implements the CollaborationMode interface \(see collaboration.go\) and is registered via RegisterMode. The engine dispatches RunSession calls to the appropriate driver based on the session's mode string.

### Current Modes

- "pair\_programming": Two agents alternate editor token in a shared workspace \(PairProgrammingDriver in collaboration\_pair\_driver.go\).
- "differential": Four\-phase A/B pipeline with independent branches and a synthesizing differentiator \(DifferentialDriver in collaboration\_diff\_driver.go\).
- "team\_parallel": N\-agent parallel teams with lead agent synthesis \(ParallelTeamDriver in collaboration\_team\_driver.go\).

The ParallelTeamDriver uses message bus topics for N\-agent coordination:

- team.\{sessionID\}.status \-\- shared task board
- team.\{sessionID\}.message \-\- inter\-agent broadcast/targeted messages
- team.\{sessionID\}.result \-\- partial results aggregation

No changes to existing modes or the CollaborationMode interface are required. The CollaborationMode interface is intentionally minimal \(Name, Run, CanInitiate\) to support heterogeneous collaboration strategies.

See docs/concepts/multi\-agent\-parallelism.md for the full design.

	type CollaborationEngine struct {
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

<a name="CollaborationMode"></a>
## type CollaborationMode

CollaborationMode is the interface for pluggable collaboration modes.

	type CollaborationMode interface {
	    Name() string
	    Run(ctx context.Context, sess *CollaborationSession) (*CollaborationResult, error)
	    CanInitiate(agentID string, reason string) bool
	}

<a name="CollaborationResult"></a>
## type CollaborationResult

CollaborationResult holds the outcome of a completed session.

	type CollaborationResult struct {
	    SessionID   string        `json:"session_id"`
	    State       SessionState  `json:"state"`
	    FinalOutput string        `json:"final_output,omitempty"`
	    Workspace   string        `json:"workspace,omitempty"`
	    TurnCount   int           `json:"turn_count"`
	    Duration    time.Duration `json:"duration"`
	}

<a name="CollaborationSession"></a>
## type CollaborationSession

CollaborationSession represents an active collaboration instance.

	type CollaborationSession struct {
	    ID           string
	    Mode         string // "pair_programming" | "differential"
	    TaskID       string // parent task
	    State        SessionState
	    Workspace    string      // base workspace path
	    Participants []string    // agent IDs involved
	    TurnLog      []TurnEntry // complete turn history
	    ParentID     string      // for nested (agent-initiated) sessions
	    TokenBudget  int64
	    TimeBudget   time.Duration
	    TurnTimeout  time.Duration // max time per turn
	    MaxTurns     int
	    CreatedAt    time.Time
	    UpdatedAt    time.Time
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

	func (s *CollaborationSession) MarkFailed()

<a name="CollaborationSession.TurnCount"></a>
### func \(\*CollaborationSession\) TurnCount

	func (s *CollaborationSession) TurnCount() int

TurnCount returns the number of completed turns.

<a name="CompressionReport"></a>
## type CompressionReport

CompressionReport contains statistics about a compression operation.

	type CompressionReport struct {
	    TokensBefore   int
	    TokensAfter    int
	    TokensRemoved  int
	    MessagesBefore int
	    MessagesAfter  int
	}

<a name="ContextTransform"></a>

	type ContextTransform struct {

	    ToolDefs []llm.ToolDefinition // replacement tool definitions (nil = keep original)
	    Modified bool
	    Reason   string
	}

<a name="Conversation"></a>
## type Conversation

Conversation manages chat message history with LRU eviction and truncation.

	type Conversation struct {
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

<a name="NewConversation"></a>
### func NewConversation

	func NewConversation(opts ...ConversationOption) *Conversation

NewConversation creates a new conversation with optional configuration.

<a name="Conversation.AddAnchorMessage"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) AddAnchorMessage

	func (c *Conversation) AddAnchorMessage(role llm.Role, content string)

AddAnchorMessage adds a message that is exempt from truncation. Anchor messages are treated as ImportanceCritical and preserved during context management. This is used for validation instructions, escalation triggers, and other critical context.

<a name="Conversation.AddAssistantMessage"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) AddAssistantMessage

	func (c *Conversation) AddAssistantMessage(content string)

AddAssistantMessage is a convenience method to add an assistant message.

<a name="Conversation.AddAssistantMessageWithToolCalls"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) AddAssistantMessageWithToolCalls

	func (c *Conversation) AddAssistantMessageWithToolCalls(content string, toolCalls []llm.ToolCall)

AddAssistantMessageWithToolCalls adds an assistant message with tool calls.

<a name="Conversation.AddMessage"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) AddMessage

	func (c *Conversation) AddMessage(msg llm.ChatMessage)

AddMessage appends a message to the conversation history.

<a name="Conversation.AddSystemMessage"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) AddSystemMessage

	func (c *Conversation) AddSystemMessage(content string)

AddSystemMessage adds a system message.

<a name="Conversation.AddToolResult"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) AddToolResult

	func (c *Conversation) AddToolResult(toolCallID, content string)

AddToolResult adds a tool result message.

<a name="Conversation.AddUserMessage"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) AddUserMessage

	func (c *Conversation) AddUserMessage(content string)

AddUserMessage is a convenience method to add a user message.

<a name="Conversation.BuildPromptWithSnapshot"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) BuildPromptWithSnapshot

	func (c *Conversation) BuildPromptWithSnapshot() string

BuildPromptWithSnapshot returns the memory context for prompt building, using the frozen snapshot if available for prefix cache efficiency.

<a name="Conversation.Clear"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) Clear

	func (c *Conversation) Clear()

Clear removes all messages except the system prompt.

<a name="Conversation.ClearAnchors"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) ClearAnchors

	func (c *Conversation) ClearAnchors()

ClearAnchors removes all anchor markings \(used when context is reset\).

<a name="Conversation.ClearMemoryContext"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) ClearMemoryContext

	func (c *Conversation) ClearMemoryContext()

ClearMemoryContext clears both live context and frozen snapshot.

<a name="Conversation.ClearMemorySnapshot"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) ClearMemorySnapshot

	func (c *Conversation) ClearMemorySnapshot()

ClearMemorySnapshot clears the frozen snapshot \(e.g., at session end\).

<a name="Conversation.Clone"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) Clone

	func (c *Conversation) Clone() *Conversation

Clone creates a deep copy of the conversation.

<a name="Conversation.CompressByImportance"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) CompressByImportance

	func (c *Conversation) CompressByImportance(targetRatio float64) CompressionReport

CompressByImportance removes messages based on semantic importance to reach a target token ratio. The targetRatio parameter \(0.0\-1.0\) specifies what fraction of the current token count to retain. For example, 0.5 means compress to 50% of current tokens.

Messages are sorted by importance \(lowest first\), then by token count \(highest first\), and removed in that order until the target is reached. Anchor messages are never removed. User input messages are treated as ImportanceCritical and preserved.

<a name="Conversation.FetchAndFreezeMemory"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) FetchAndFreezeMemory

	func (c *Conversation) FetchAndFreezeMemory(ctx context.Context, fetchFn func(ctx context.Context) (string, error)) error

FetchAndFreezeMemory fetches memory context and freezes it for prefix caching. The fetchFn should fetch the relevant memory context and return it as a string. Returns an error if the fetch fails.

<a name="Conversation.FindMessageByContent"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) FindMessageByContent

	func (c *Conversation) FindMessageByContent(contentPrefix string) int

FindMessageByContent finds the index of a message matching the given content prefix. Returns \-1 if not found.

<a name="Conversation.FreezeMemorySnapshot"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) FreezeMemorySnapshot

	func (c *Conversation) FreezeMemorySnapshot(_ context.Context) error

FreezeMemorySnapshot captures the current memory context for prefix caching. This implements the Hermes pattern where memory is frozen at session start to enable API prefix caching. The snapshot remains stable for the session, allowing the LLM API to cache the prefix and reduce token costs. Returns an error if memory context is empty \(nothing to freeze\).

<a name="Conversation.GetCachePrefixHash"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) GetCachePrefixHash

	func (c *Conversation) GetCachePrefixHash() string

GetCachePrefixHash returns the current cache prefix hash for external cache invalidation checks.

<a name="Conversation.GetCompactionCandidates"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) GetCompactionCandidates

	func (c *Conversation) GetCompactionCandidates(targetRatio float64) (candidates []int, report CompressionReport)

GetCompactionCandidates returns the indices of messages that would be compressed at the given target ratio, without actually removing them. The caller uses this to decide whether to trigger compaction and to know which messages are affected. Returns indices sorted by importance \(lowest importance first among the candidates\) and a CompressionReport describing what the compaction would achieve.

<a name="Conversation.GetMemoryContext"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) GetMemoryContext

	func (c *Conversation) GetMemoryContext() string

GetMemoryContext returns the live memory context \(deprecated, use snapshot for prompts\).

<a name="Conversation.GetMemorySnapshot"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) GetMemorySnapshot

	func (c *Conversation) GetMemorySnapshot() string

GetMemorySnapshot returns the frozen memory snapshot for prefix caching. Returns empty string if no snapshot has been frozen.

<a name="Conversation.GetMessages"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) GetMessages

	func (c *Conversation) GetMessages() []llm.ChatMessage

GetMessages returns all messages in the conversation. The first message is always the system prompt if set.

<a name="Conversation.GetSystemPrompt"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) GetSystemPrompt

	func (c *Conversation) GetSystemPrompt() string

GetSystemPrompt returns the current system prompt.

<a name="Conversation.GetWindowedMessages"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) GetWindowedMessages

	func (c *Conversation) GetWindowedMessages(tokenBudget int) []llm.ChatMessage

GetWindowedMessages returns messages within a token budget with smart context selection. It preserves: \(1\) system prompt always, \(2\) original user message, \(3\) anchor messages, \(4\) most recent messages. Anchor messages are always included regardless of token budget. Returns messages that fit within the token budget.

<a name="Conversation.HasMemorySnapshot"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) HasMemorySnapshot

	func (c *Conversation) HasMemorySnapshot() bool

HasMemorySnapshot returns true if a memory snapshot has been frozen.

<a name="Conversation.InjectContext"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) InjectContext

	func (c *Conversation) InjectContext(ctxStr string)

This is used for memory injection before LLM calls.

<a name="Conversation.InjectContextBounded"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) InjectContextBounded

	func (c *Conversation) InjectContextBounded(ctxStr string, maxTokens int)

InjectContextBounded inserts context with a token budget limit. This is used for memory injection to prevent memory from dominating the context. If the context exceeds the budget, it is truncated proportionally.

<a name="Conversation.IsAnchorMessage"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) IsAnchorMessage

	func (c *Conversation) IsAnchorMessage(content string) bool

IsAnchorMessage checks if a message content is anchored.

<a name="Conversation.LastMessage"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) LastMessage

	func (c *Conversation) LastMessage() *llm.ChatMessage

LastMessage returns the most recent message, or nil if empty.

<a name="Conversation.Len"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) Len

	func (c *Conversation) Len() int

Len returns the number of messages \(excluding system prompt\).

<a name="Conversation.PrefixChanged"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) PrefixChanged

	func (c *Conversation) PrefixChanged() bool

PrefixChanged returns true if the system prompt \+ tools hash has changed since the last call to StabilizeToolPrefix. Returns true on the first call \(when no hash has been computed yet\).

<a name="Conversation.RemoveCompactedMessages"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) RemoveCompactedMessages

	func (c *Conversation) RemoveCompactedMessages(indices []int) int

RemoveCompactedMessages removes the messages at the given indices from the in\-memory conversation. The indices must be valid positions in the current message slice. After removal, the messageTypes slice is kept in sync. Returns the approximate tokens saved by the removal.

<a name="Conversation.RemoveLast"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) RemoveLast

	func (c *Conversation) RemoveLast() *llm.ChatMessage

RemoveLast removes and returns the last message.

<a name="Conversation.RestoreFromMessages"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) RestoreFromMessages

	func (c *Conversation) RestoreFromMessages(messages []llm.ChatMessage)

RestoreFromMessages replaces the conversation's message history with the provided messages. Preserves system prompt and other config. Re\-classifies all messages for importance tracking.

<a name="Conversation.SetBranchPoint"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) SetBranchPoint

	func (c *Conversation) SetBranchPoint(index int)

SetBranchPoint truncates the conversation to the message at the given index. All messages after the index are removed. Used when branching back to a prior message.

<a name="Conversation.SetMemoryContext"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) SetMemoryContext

	func (c *Conversation) SetMemoryContext(ctxStr string)

SetMemoryContext sets the live memory context \(for internal use\).

<a name="Conversation.SetSystemPrompt"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) SetSystemPrompt

	func (c *Conversation) SetSystemPrompt(prompt string)

SetSystemPrompt sets or updates the system prompt.

<a name="Conversation.StabilizeToolPrefix"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) StabilizeToolPrefix

	func (c *Conversation) StabilizeToolPrefix(tools []llm.ToolDefinition) []llm.ToolDefinition

StabilizeToolPrefix creates a deterministic byte layout for tool definitions and updates the cache prefix hash. Tools are sorted alphabetically by name, and each tool is serialized as name \+ description \+ parameters sorted by key. The resulting SHA256 hash enables detection of prefix changes for cache invalidation. Returns the stabilized tool list sorted by name.

<a name="Conversation.Truncate"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) Truncate

	func (c *Conversation) Truncate() int

Truncate removes old messages when the conversation exceeds the maximum. It preserves the system prompt and keeps the most recent messages. This implements LRU\-style eviction for conversation history.

<a name="Conversation.TruncateByImportance"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) TruncateByImportance

	func (c *Conversation) TruncateByImportance(tokenBudget int) int

TruncateByImportance removes messages based on semantic importance rather than recency. It preserves messages in priority order: 1. System prompt \(always\) 2. Original user message \(critical\) 3. Assistant conclusions/summaries \(high\) 4. Tool results with key findings \(high\) 5. Assistant plans \(medium\) 6. Regular tool results \(medium\) 7. Intermediate reasoning steps \(low \- removed first\) Anchor messages are always treated as ImportanceCritical and preserved. Returns the number of messages removed.

<a name="Conversation.TruncateByTokens"></a>
### func \(\*Conversation\) TruncateByTokens

	func (c *Conversation) TruncateByTokens(tokenBudget int) int

TruncateByTokens removes old messages to fit within a token budget. It uses a rough estimate of 3 characters per token \(appropriate for JSON/code\-heavy content\). It counts both Content and ToolCalls fields for accurate estimation. Anchor messages are preserved and never removed. Returns the number of messages removed.

<a name="ConversationStore.GetOrRestore"></a>
### func \(\*ConversationStore\) GetOrRestore

	func (s *ConversationStore) GetOrRestore(id string, restoreFn func() ([]llm.ChatMessage, error)) (*Conversation, error)

GetOrRestore retrieves or restores a conversation from persistence. If the conversation is already in the store, it is returned directly. Otherwise, restoreFn is called to load messages from the backing store. On restore failure, an empty conversation is created and the error is returned.

<a name="DetectionConfig"></a>

	type DetectionConfig struct {
	    // CycleDetection: minimum consecutive similar tool calls to trigger
	    CycleThreshold int
	
	    // ConvergenceDetection: minimum consecutive similar responses to trigger
	    ConvergenceThreshold int
	
	    // HistorySize: how many iterations to keep in history
	    HistorySize int
	}

<a name="DispatchResult"></a>
## type DispatchResult

DispatchResult is the result of dispatching a request.

	type DispatchResult struct {
	    // Task is the created task if any.
	    Task *task.Task `json:"task,omitempty"`
	    // AgentID is the agent that will handle the request.
	    AgentID string `json:"agent_id"`
	    // Intent is the classified intent.
	    Intent *Intent `json:"intent"`
	    // Response is the direct response if no agent delegation needed.
	    Response string `json:"response,omitempty"`
	    // MemoryContext are memories retrieved for context.
	    MemoryContext []memory.MemoryResult `json:"memory_context,omitempty"`
	    // Steps are step summaries for the ACK message.
	    Steps []TaskStepSummary `json:"steps,omitempty"`
	    // ExplicitSteerMode indicates the user pressed ctrl+s to force steering.
	    ExplicitSteerMode bool `json:"explicit_steer_mode,omitempty"`
	
	    // OriginalInput is the full, untruncated user input. Preserved so that
	    // agents receive the complete message rather than a ~100-char summary.
	    OriginalInput string `json:"original_input,omitempty"`
	
	    // ModelDirective is the model reassignment directive if user specified one
	    ModelDirective *ModelReassignmentDirective `json:"model_directive,omitempty"`
	    // ClarificationReply is the clarification question if directive is ambiguous
	    ClarificationReply string `json:"clarification_reply,omitempty"`
	    // ClarificationNeeded indicates the directive needs user clarification
	    ClarificationNeeded bool `json:"clarification_needed,omitempty"`
	    // Plan is the created plan if plan routing was triggered.
	    Plan *plan.Plan `json:"plan,omitempty"`
	}

<a name="Dispatcher"></a>
## type Dispatcher

Dispatcher handles intake classification and routing of requests.

	type Dispatcher struct {
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

<a name="NewDispatcher"></a>
### func NewDispatcher

	func NewDispatcher(cfg DispatcherConfig) *Dispatcher

NewDispatcher creates a new dispatcher.

<a name="Dispatcher.ClassifyAndRoute"></a>
### func \(\*Dispatcher\) ClassifyAndRoute

	func (d *Dispatcher) ClassifyAndRoute(ctx context.Context, input, sessionID string) (*DispatchResult, error)

ClassifyAndRoute is the main entry point for the dispatcher.

<a name="Dispatcher.FollowUpActiveAgent"></a>
### func \(\*Dispatcher\) FollowUpActiveAgent

	func (d *Dispatcher) FollowUpActiveAgent(ctx context.Context, conversationID, content, source string) error

FollowUpActiveAgent sends a follow\-up message to an active agent for the given conversation. This waits for the agent to reach a natural stopping point. Returns ErrQueueNotFound if no active queue exists.

<a name="Dispatcher.GetActiveTasks"></a>
### func \(\*Dispatcher\) GetActiveTasks

	func (d *Dispatcher) GetActiveTasks(ctx context.Context) ([]*task.Task, error)

GetCapabilityMatcher returns the capability matcher for external access.

GetFallbackDetails returns recent fallback entries for analysis.

<a name="Dispatcher.GetInterruptStatus"></a>

GetInterruptStatus returns the interrupt status for a task. Returns \(isInterrupted, reason, message\) or an error if the task is not found.

<a name="Dispatcher.GetPendingAmendments"></a>

<a name="Dispatcher.GetTask"></a>

	func (d *Dispatcher) GetTask(ctx context.Context, taskID string) (*task.Task, error)

GetTask returns a task by ID.

<a name="Dispatcher.ProcessAmendment"></a>
### func \(\*Dispatcher\) ProcessAmendment

	func (d *Dispatcher) ProcessAmendment(ctx context.Context, requestID string) (*task.AmendmentReply, error)

ProcessAmendment processes a pending amendment request.

<a name="Dispatcher.ResumeAfterClarification"></a>
### func \(\*Dispatcher\) ResumeAfterClarification

	func (d *Dispatcher) ResumeAfterClarification(ctx context.Context, originalInput, userResponse, sessionID string) (*DispatchResult, error)

ResumeAfterClarification re\-classifies a user input that is a response to a previous clarification request. It combines the original input with the user's response and re\-runs intent analysis. If the combined input is still ambiguous, it asks follow\-up questions. Otherwise, it routes normally.

<a name="Dispatcher.RouteToAgent"></a>
### func \(\*Dispatcher\) RouteToAgent

	func (d *Dispatcher) RouteToAgent(ctx context.Context, result *DispatchResult, conversationID string) (string, error)

RouteToAgent routes a dispatch result to the appropriate agent. If an active agent loop exists for this conversation, it injects the message into the queue \(steer or follow\-up\) based on the SteeringHeuristicTable. Otherwise, it runs the agent synchronously.

<a name="Dispatcher.SetCapabilityMatcher"></a>
### func \(\*Dispatcher\) SetCapabilityMatcher

	func (d *Dispatcher) SetCapabilityMatcher(matcher *CapabilityMatcher)

SetCapabilityMatcher sets the capability matcher for fast routing.

<a name="Dispatcher.ShouldDispatchAsync"></a>
### func \(\*Dispatcher\) ShouldDispatchAsync

	func (d *Dispatcher) ShouldDispatchAsync(result *DispatchResult) bool

ShouldDispatchAsync returns true if the dispatch result should be handled asynchronously via the orchestrator pipeline rather than inline.

<a name="Dispatcher.ShouldRouteToCollaborate"></a>
### func \(\*Dispatcher\) ShouldRouteToCollaborate

	func (d *Dispatcher) ShouldRouteToCollaborate(result *DispatchResult) bool

ShouldRouteToCollaborate returns true if the dispatch result should be routed to the CollaborationEngine for a collaboration session.

<a name="Dispatcher.ShouldRouteToPair"></a>
### func \(\*Dispatcher\) ShouldRouteToPair

	func (d *Dispatcher) ShouldRouteToPair(result *DispatchResult) bool

ShouldRouteToPair returns true if the dispatch result should use channel\-based pairing instead of the step\-based orchestrator.

<a name="Dispatcher.SteerActiveAgent"></a>
### func \(\*Dispatcher\) SteerActiveAgent

	func (d *Dispatcher) SteerActiveAgent(ctx context.Context, conversationID, content, source string) error

SteerActiveAgent sends a steering message to an active agent for the given conversation. This interrupts the current flow. Returns ErrQueueNotFound if no active queue exists.

<a name="Dispatcher.SubmitAmendment"></a>
### func \(\*Dispatcher\) SubmitAmendment

	func (d *Dispatcher) SubmitAmendment(ctx context.Context, taskID string, amendmentType task.AmendmentType, content string, metadata map[string]any) (*task.AmendmentRequest, error)

SubmitAmendment submits an amendment request for a task.

<a name="Dispatcher.ValidateRouting"></a>

ValidateRouting compares the routed agent against expected.

<a name="DispatcherConfig"></a>
## type DispatcherConfig

DispatcherConfig holds configuration for creating a Dispatcher.

	type DispatcherConfig struct {
	    Registry          *AgentRegistry
	    MemvidClient      *memvid.Client
	    MemoryMgr         *memory.Manager
	    TaskStore         *task.Store
	    TaskRegistry      *task.Registry
	    AmendmentManager  *task.AmendmentManager
	    SkillRegistry     *skills.Registry
	    SkillExecutor     *skills.Executor
	    TemplateRegistry  *templates.Registry
	    Logger            *slog.Logger
	    LLMClient         *llm.Client
	    ClassifierClient  *llm.Client // Separate client for classification (nil = use LLMClient)
	    ClassifierModel   string
	    ClassifierTimeout time.Duration // Per-classification timeout; 0 = defaultClassifierTimeout (10s).
	    CapabilityMatcher *CapabilityMatcher
	    EmbeddingClient   EmbeddingClient
	    SessionMaxAge     time.Duration
	    PlanManager       *plan.PlanManager
	}

<a name="DrainMode"></a>
## type DrainMode

DrainMode controls how a follow\-up queue drains its messages.

	type DrainMode string

	    DrainAll DrainMode = "all"
	    DrainOne DrainMode = "one"
	)

<a name="ParseDrainMode"></a>

<a name="EmbeddingClient"></a>
## type EmbeddingClient

EmbeddingClient generates vector embeddings for text.

	type EmbeddingClient interface {
	    Embed(ctx context.Context, text string) ([]float64, error)
	    EmbedBatch(ctx context.Context, texts []string) ([][]float64, error)
	    Dimension() int
	}

<a name="ErrorPayload"></a>
## type ErrorPayload

ErrorPayload is the payload for "error" type messages.

	type ErrorPayload struct {
	    // Code is an error code.
	    Code string `json:"code"`
	
	    // Message is a human-readable error message.
	    Message string `json:"message"`
	
	    // Details contains additional error context.
	    Details map[string]any `json:"details,omitempty"`
	
	    // Recoverable indicates if the error can be retried.
	    Recoverable bool `json:"recoverable"`
	}

<a name="EscalationConfig"></a>
## type EscalationConfig

EscalationConfig holds configuration for the escalation manager.

	type EscalationConfig struct {
	    // Enabled turns on escalation handling
	    Enabled bool
	    // MaxEscalationLevels is the maximum re-planning levels before human notification (default: 3)
	    MaxEscalationLevels int
	    // NotificationTopic is the bus topic for human intervention notifications
	    NotificationTopic string
	}

	func DefaultEscalationConfig() EscalationConfig

DefaultEscalationConfig returns sensible defaults.

	}

<a name="EscalationManager"></a>
## type EscalationManager

EscalationManager handles task escalation and re\-planning when tasks fail.

	type EscalationManager struct {
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

<a name="NewEscalationManager"></a>
### func NewEscalationManager

	func NewEscalationManager(cfg EscalationManagerConfig) *EscalationManager

NewEscalationManager creates a new escalation manager.

<a name="EscalationManager.ClearEscalation"></a>
### func \(\*EscalationManager\) ClearEscalation

	func (em *EscalationManager) ClearEscalation(taskID string)

ClearEscalation removes escalation tracking for a completed task.

<a name="EscalationManager.Escalate"></a>
### func \(\*EscalationManager\) Escalate

	func (em *EscalationManager) Escalate(ctx context.Context, failure FailureContext) error

Escalate triggers re\-planning for a failed task. It checks the current escalation level and either re\-plans into smaller tasks or notifies for human intervention if the max level has been reached.

<a name="EscalationManager.EscalateForValidation"></a>
### func \(\*EscalationManager\) EscalateForValidation

	func (em *EscalationManager) EscalateForValidation(ctx context.Context, failure FailureContext, validationLoops int) error

EscalateForValidation triggers escalation specifically for validation failures. Validation failures get a dedicated escalation path with more context.

<a name="EscalationManager.GetEscalationLevel"></a>
### func \(\*EscalationManager\) GetEscalationLevel

	func (em *EscalationManager) GetEscalationLevel(taskID string) int

GetEscalationLevel returns the current escalation level for a task.

<a name="EventEmitter"></a>
## type EventEmitter

EventEmitter publishes typed agent events to in\-process listeners and bridges them to the message bus for system\-wide subscribers.

Typed events are the source of truth for agent lifecycle concerns. The bus bridge translates them into bus messages so existing subscribers continue to work during migration.

	type EventEmitter struct {
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

<a name="NewEventEmitter"></a>
### func NewEventEmitter

	func NewEventEmitter(agentID string, b *bus.MessageBus, logger *slog.Logger) *EventEmitter

NewEventEmitter creates a new EventEmitter for the given agent. If bus is nil, the emitter operates without bus bridging \(typed listeners only\).

<a name="EventEmitter.Emit"></a>
### func \(\*EventEmitter\) Emit

	func (e *EventEmitter) Emit(ctx context.Context, eventType AgentEventType, data AgentEventData)

Emit publishes a typed event to all matching listeners and bridges to the bus. Sync listeners run inline. Async listeners run in goroutines tracked for settlement.

<a name="EventEmitter.EmitWithFields"></a>
### func \(\*EventEmitter\) EmitWithFields

	func (e *EventEmitter) EmitWithFields(ctx context.Context, event AgentEvent)

EmitWithFields publishes an event with explicit metadata fields. This is used when the caller needs to set ConversationID or Iteration.

<a name="EventEmitter.Off"></a>
### func \(\*EventEmitter\) Off

	func (e *EventEmitter) Off(name string)

Off removes a listener by name from all event types and the all\-listeners list.

<a name="EventEmitter.On"></a>
### func \(\*EventEmitter\) On

	func (e *EventEmitter) On(eventType AgentEventType, name string, listener EventListener)

On registers a synchronous listener for a specific event type. The listener is called inline during Emit. Name must be unique for removal.

<a name="EventEmitter.OnAll"></a>
### func \(\*EventEmitter\) OnAll

	func (e *EventEmitter) OnAll(name string, listener EventListener)

OnAll registers a listener that receives all events. The listener is called synchronously during Emit.

<a name="EventEmitter.OnAsync"></a>
### func \(\*EventEmitter\) OnAsync

	func (e *EventEmitter) OnAsync(eventType AgentEventType, name string, listener EventListener)

OnAsync registers an asynchronous listener for a specific event type. The listener runs in a goroutine tracked by the settlement WaitGroup. Name must be unique for removal.

<a name="EventEmitter.WaitForIdle"></a>
### func \(\*EventEmitter\) WaitForIdle

	func (e *EventEmitter) WaitForIdle(ctx context.Context) error

WaitForIdle blocks until all async listeners from recent Emit calls have finished processing. Returns ctx.Err\(\) if the context expires.

<a name="EventListener"></a>
## type EventListener

EventListener is a callback for typed agent events.

	type EventListener func(ctx context.Context, event AgentEvent)

<a name="ExecutionResult"></a>
## type ExecutionResult

ExecutionResult represents the result of a tool execution.

	type ExecutionResult struct {
	    ToolCallID string            `json:"tool_call_id"`
	    Success    bool              `json:"success"`
	    Result     any               `json:"result,omitempty"`
	    Error      string            `json:"error,omitempty"`
	    Cached     bool              `json:"cached,omitempty"`    // True if result came from cache
	    Evidence   []models.Evidence `json:"evidence,omitempty"`  // Evidence of tool side-effects
	    Terminate  bool              `json:"terminate,omitempty"` // Advisory: hint that result is final and needs no LLM follow-up
	}

<a name="ExecutionResult.ToChatMessage"></a>
### func \(\*ExecutionResult\) ToChatMessage

	func (r *ExecutionResult) ToChatMessage() llm.ChatMessage

ToChatMessage converts the result to a tool role chat message.

<a name="ExecutionResult.ToCompressedJSON"></a>
### func \(\*ExecutionResult\) ToCompressedJSON

	func (r *ExecutionResult) ToCompressedJSON(maxTokens int) string

ToCompressedJSON converts the result to a JSON string, compressing if over maxTokens. Uses 3 chars/token estimation \(appropriate for JSON/code content\). Large results are truncated with a summary.

<a name="ExecutionResult.ToJSON"></a>
### func \(\*ExecutionResult\) ToJSON

	func (r *ExecutionResult) ToJSON() string

ToJSON converts the result to a JSON string.

	}

	func (e *Executor) ExecuteAll(ctx context.Context, toolCalls []llm.ToolCall) []*ExecutionResult

	func (e *Executor) ExecuteSequential(ctx context.Context, toolCalls []llm.ToolCall) []*ExecutionResult

### func \(\*Executor\) SetRegistry

	func (e *Executor) SetRegistry(registry ToolRegistry)

SetRegistry updates the tool registry used by this executor. This is used when the registry needs to be swapped \(e.g., for skill execution with filtered tools\). AGENT\-6 fix.

<a name="FailureContext"></a>

	type FailureContext struct {
	    TaskID       string    `json:"task_id"`
	    StepID       string    `json:"step_id"`
	    AgentID      string    `json:"agent_id"`
	    Error        string    `json:"error"`
	    Stage        string    `json:"stage"` // which stage failed
	    RetryCount   int       `json:"retry_count"`
	    PartialState string    `json:"partial_state,omitempty"`
	    Timestamp    time.Time `json:"timestamp"`
	}

<a name="HallucinationConfig"></a>
## type HallucinationConfig

HallucinationConfig configures the hallucination detector.

	type HallucinationConfig struct {
	    // Enabled turns on hallucination detection
	    Enabled bool
	    // Sensitivity determines detection aggressiveness (default: "low")
	    Sensitivity HallucinationSensitivity
	    // MaxIndicators is the number of indicators needed to trigger recovery (default: 2)
	    MaxIndicators int
	}

	}

<a name="HallucinationIndicator"></a>
## type HallucinationIndicator

HallucinationIndicator represents a single detected hallucination indicator.

	type HallucinationIndicator struct {
	    Type        string  `json:"type"` // "confident_claim", "fabricated_ref", "contradiction", "impossible_response"
	    Description string  `json:"description"`
	    Confidence  float64 `json:"confidence"` // 0.0-1.0 confidence this is actually a hallucination
	    Content     string  `json:"content"`    // The specific text that triggered detection
	}

<a name="HallucinationResult"></a>
## type HallucinationResult

HallucinationResult holds the results of hallucination analysis.

	type HallucinationResult struct {
	    Indicators    []HallucinationIndicator `json:"indicators"`
	    Score         float64                  `json:"score"`          // 0.0-1.0 overall hallucination probability
	    ShouldRecover bool                     `json:"should_recover"` // true if MaxIndicators threshold exceeded
	}

<a name="HallucinationSensitivity"></a>
## type HallucinationSensitivity

HallucinationSensitivity determines how aggressively to detect hallucinations.

	type HallucinationSensitivity string

<a name="SensitivityLow"></a>

	const (
	    SensitivityLow    HallucinationSensitivity = "low"
	    SensitivityMedium HallucinationSensitivity = "medium"
	    SensitivityHigh   HallucinationSensitivity = "high"
	)

<a name="HandoffRequest"></a>

HandoffRequest represents a handoff request payload from the request\_handoff tool.

	type HandoffRequest struct {
	    TaskID        string `json:"task_id"`
	    FromStepID    string `json:"from_step_id"`
	    FromAgentID   string `json:"from_agent_id"`
	    ToAgentID     string `json:"to_agent_id"`
	    Description   string `json:"description"`
	    ToolHint      string `json:"tool_hint,omitempty"`
	    Reason        string `json:"reason,omitempty"`
	    PartialResult string `json:"partial_result,omitempty"`
	    InjectAfter   bool   `json:"inject_after"`
	}

<a name="HookPriority"></a>
## type HookPriority

HookPriority defines ordering for hook execution. Lower values run first.

	type HookPriority int

<a name="HookPriorityCritical"></a>

	const (
	    HookPriorityCritical HookPriority = 0   // security, must run first
	    HookPriorityHigh     HookPriority = 10  // audit, cost gating
	    HookPriorityNormal   HookPriority = 50  // default
	    HookPriorityLow      HookPriority = 90  // logging, metrics
	    HookPriorityMonitor  HookPriority = 100 // monitoring, shadow training
	)

<a name="HookRegistration"></a>
## type HookRegistration

HookRegistration holds a registered hook with its priority.

	type HookRegistration struct {
	    Name     string
	    Priority HookPriority
	    Hook     any // concrete hook interface
	}

<a name="HookRegistry"></a>
## type HookRegistry

HookRegistry manages all agent hooks. Hooks are executed in priority order \(lowest first\) with short\-circuit semantics for blocking hooks.

	type HookRegistry struct {
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

<a name="NewHookRegistry"></a>
### func NewHookRegistry

	func NewHookRegistry(logger *slog.Logger) *HookRegistry

NewHookRegistry creates a new HookRegistry.

<a name="HookRegistry.RegisterAfterToolCall"></a>
### func \(\*HookRegistry\) RegisterAfterToolCall

	func (r *HookRegistry) RegisterAfterToolCall(name string, priority HookPriority, hook AfterToolCallHook)

RegisterAfterToolCall registers an AfterToolCallHook.

<a name="HookRegistry.RegisterBeforeToolCall"></a>
### func \(\*HookRegistry\) RegisterBeforeToolCall

	func (r *HookRegistry) RegisterBeforeToolCall(name string, priority HookPriority, hook BeforeToolCallHook)

RegisterBeforeToolCall registers a BeforeToolCallHook.

<a name="HookRegistry.RegisterPrepareNextTurn"></a>
### func \(\*HookRegistry\) RegisterPrepareNextTurn

	func (r *HookRegistry) RegisterPrepareNextTurn(name string, priority HookPriority, hook PrepareNextTurnHook)

RegisterPrepareNextTurn registers a PrepareNextTurnHook.

<a name="HookRegistry.RegisterShouldStopAfterTurn"></a>
### func \(\*HookRegistry\) RegisterShouldStopAfterTurn

	func (r *HookRegistry) RegisterShouldStopAfterTurn(name string, priority HookPriority, hook ShouldStopAfterTurnHook)

RegisterShouldStopAfterTurn registers a ShouldStopAfterTurnHook.

<a name="HookRegistry.RegisterTransformContext"></a>
### func \(\*HookRegistry\) RegisterTransformContext

	func (r *HookRegistry) RegisterTransformContext(name string, priority HookPriority, hook TransformContextHook)

RegisterTransformContext registers a TransformContextHook.

<a name="HookRegistry.RunAfterToolCalls"></a>
### func \(\*HookRegistry\) RunAfterToolCalls

	func (r *HookRegistry) RunAfterToolCalls(ctx context.Context, toolCall llm.ToolCall, result *ExecutionResult) OverrideResult

RunAfterToolCalls runs all AfterToolCall hooks in priority order. Returns the first OverrideResult with Override=true \(short\-circuit\).

<a name="HookRegistry.RunBeforeToolCalls"></a>
### func \(\*HookRegistry\) RunBeforeToolCalls

	func (r *HookRegistry) RunBeforeToolCalls(ctx context.Context, toolCall llm.ToolCall) BlockResult

RunBeforeToolCalls runs all BeforeToolCall hooks in priority order. Returns the first BlockResult with Block=true \(short\-circuit\).

<a name="HookRegistry.RunPrepareNextTurn"></a>
### func \(\*HookRegistry\) RunPrepareNextTurn

	func (r *HookRegistry) RunPrepareNextTurn(ctx context.Context, state TurnState) TurnModification

RunPrepareNextTurn runs all PrepareNextTurn hooks in priority order. Returns the first TurnModification with Modified=true \(short\-circuit\).

<a name="HookRegistry.RunShouldStopAfterTurn"></a>
### func \(\*HookRegistry\) RunShouldStopAfterTurn

	func (r *HookRegistry) RunShouldStopAfterTurn(ctx context.Context, state TurnState) StopDecision

RunShouldStopAfterTurn runs all ShouldStopAfterTurn hooks in priority order. Returns the first StopDecision with Stop=true \(short\-circuit\).

<a name="HookRegistry.RunTransformContext"></a>
### func \(\*HookRegistry\) RunTransformContext

	func (r *HookRegistry) RunTransformContext(ctx context.Context, messages []llm.ChatMessage, toolDefs []llm.ToolDefinition) ContextTransform

RunTransformContext runs all TransformContext hooks in priority order. Returns the first ContextTransform with Modified=true \(short\-circuit\).

<a name="HookRegistry.Unregister"></a>
### func \(\*HookRegistry\) Unregister

	func (r *HookRegistry) Unregister(name string)

Unregister removes all hooks with the given name.

<a name="Intent"></a>
## type Intent

Intent represents the classified intent of a user message.

	type Intent struct {
	    // Type is the high-level intent category.
	    Type string `json:"type"`
	    // Confidence is the confidence score [0.0, 1.0].
	    Confidence float64 `json:"confidence"`
	    // AgentType is the specialist agent to route to.
	    AgentType string `json:"agent_type"`
	    // MemoryRefs are relevant memory IDs to pass along.
	    MemoryRefs []string `json:"memory_refs,omitempty"`
	    // RequiresPlanning indicates if the task needs planning first.
	    RequiresPlanning bool `json:"requires_planning"`
	    // Summary is a brief description of the intent.
	    Summary string `json:"summary,omitempty"`
	    // TrueAnalysis holds the IntentGate-style pre-classification analysis if available.
	    TrueAnalysis *TrueIntentAnalysis `json:"true_analysis,omitempty"`
	}

<a name="Intent.MarshalJSON"></a>
### func \(\*Intent\) MarshalJSON

	func (i *Intent) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error)

MarshalJSON implements json.Marshaler for Intent.

IntentCategory groups intents by routing behavior.

	type IntentCategory string

<a name="CategoryInline"></a>

	    CategoryInline IntentCategory = "inline"
	    CategoryDefer  IntentCategory = "defer"
	)

<a name="IntentClassifier"></a>

IntentClassifier is an interface for classifying intents.

	type IntentClassifier interface {
	    Classify(ctx context.Context, input string, memCtx *MemoryContext) (*Intent, error)

<a name="IntentEntry"></a>
## type IntentEntry

IntentEntry represents an intent definition for indexing.

	type IntentEntry struct {
	    IntentType  IntentType
	    Description string
	    Keywords    []string
	}

<a name="IntentType"></a>
## type IntentType

IntentType represents a classified user intent.

	type IntentType string

<a name="IntentUnknown"></a>

	const (
	    // Unknown
	    IntentUnknown IntentType = "unknown"
	
	    // Conversational (inline handling)
	    IntentChat     IntentType = "chat"
	    IntentReport   IntentType = "report"
	    IntentRecall   IntentType = "recall"
	    IntentPlatform IntentType = "platform"
	    IntentStatus   IntentType = "status"
	
	    // Execution (async to orchestrator)
	    IntentCode     IntentType = "code"
	    IntentDebug    IntentType = "debug"
	    IntentReview   IntentType = "review"
	    IntentPlan     IntentType = "plan"
	    IntentGit      IntentType = "git"
	    IntentSchedule IntentType = "schedule"
	
	    // Analysis (inline)
	    IntentAnalyze  IntentType = "analyze"
	    IntentSearch   IntentType = "search"
	    IntentResearch IntentType = "research"
	
	    // Security / Tool control
	    IntentSecurity IntentType = "security"
	    IntentToolUse  IntentType = "tooluse"
	
	    // Pair channel (dual-agent conversation)
	    IntentPair IntentType = "pair"
	
	    // Collaboration (peer/differential modes)
	    IntentCollaborate IntentType = "collaborate"
	
	    // Skill invocation
	    IntentSkill IntentType = "skill"
	
	    // Compound (multi-intent)
	    IntentCompound IntentType = "compound"
	
	    // Clarification (inline)
	    IntentClarify IntentType = "clarify"
	)

<a name="IntentType.Category"></a>
### func \(IntentType\) Category

	func (t IntentType) Category() IntentCategory

Category returns the routing category for an intent.

<a name="IntentType.DefaultAgent"></a>
### func \(IntentType\) DefaultAgent

	func (t IntentType) DefaultAgent() string

DefaultAgent returns the default agent for an intent.

<a name="IntentType.Keywords"></a>
### func \(IntentType\) Keywords

	func (t IntentType) Keywords() []string

Keywords returns common trigger phrases for documentation/logging.

<a name="IntentType.RequiresPlanning"></a>
### func \(IntentType\) RequiresPlanning

	func (t IntentType) RequiresPlanning() bool

RequiresPlanning returns true if the intent benefits from orchestration.

<a name="IntentType.ShouldCreateTask"></a>
### func \(IntentType\) ShouldCreateTask

	func (t IntentType) ShouldCreateTask() bool

ShouldCreateTask returns true if the intent should create a trackable task.

<a name="IntentType.ShouldDispatchAsync"></a>
### func \(IntentType\) ShouldDispatchAsync

	func (t IntentType) ShouldDispatchAsync(requiresPlanning bool) bool

ShouldDispatchAsync returns true if the intent should be dispatched asynchronously.

<a name="LearnedPattern"></a>

	    Domain      string
	    Description string
	    Pattern     string
	    Confidence  float64
	}

<a name="LearningPipeline"></a>
## type LearningPipeline

LearningPipeline is the interface for the learning pipeline.

	type LearningPipeline interface {
	    Judge(ctx context.Context, trajectory Trajectory) (*JudgmentResult, error)
	    Distill(ctx context.Context, trajectory Trajectory, judgment *JudgmentResult) ([]*LearnedPattern, error)
	    StorePattern(ctx context.Context, pattern *LearnedPattern) error
	    Retrieve(ctx context.Context, query string, domain string, k int) ([]*LearnedPattern, error)
	}

	    Debug(msg string, args ...any)
	    Warn(msg string, args ...any)
	    Error(msg string, args ...any)
	    Info(msg string, args ...any)
	}

<a name="WithModelRef"></a>

	func WithModelRef(modelRef string) LoopOption

<a name="WithPrefetchCallback"></a>
### func WithPrefetchCallback

	func WithPrefetchCallback(callback func(query string, maxItems int)) LoopOption

WithPrefetchCallback sets the callback for prefetching memory context. This implements the Hermes pattern where context is prefetched at turn completion for the next turn, enabling background retrieval and reduced latency.

<a name="WithSkillLoader"></a>

<a name="WithTTSRManager"></a>
### func WithTTSRManager

	func WithTTSRManager(mgr *TTSRManager) LoopOption

WithTTSRManager sets the TT\-SR manager for mid\-stream rule enforcement. When enabled, each streaming delta is checked against loaded rules. If a rule matches, the stream is aborted and the rule content is retried on the next reasoning cycle.

<a name="MCPServerInfo"></a>

MCPServerInfo describes a connected MCP server for system prompt context.

	    Name      string
	    Connected bool
	    ToolCount int
	}

<a name="MatchResult"></a>
## type MatchResult

MatchResult holds the result of a capability match.

	type MatchResult struct {
	    // AgentID is the matched agent.
	    AgentID string `json:"agent_id"`
	    // Confidence is the match confidence [0.0, 1.0].
	    Confidence float64 `json:"confidence"`
	    // MatchType indicates how the match was made.
	    MatchType string `json:"match_type"`
	    // IntentType is the detected intent type.
	    IntentType string `json:"intent_type"`
	    // MatchedKeywords are the keywords that contributed to the match.
	    MatchedKeywords []string `json:"matched_keywords,omitempty"`
	    // MatchedSkill is the skill that matched (if any).
	    MatchedSkill string `json:"matched_skill,omitempty"`
	}

<a name="MemberStatus"></a>
## type MemberStatus

MemberStatus represents the execution status of a team member.

	type MemberStatus string

<a name="MemberPending"></a>

	const (
	    MemberPending MemberStatus = "pending"
	    MemberRunning MemberStatus = "running"
	    MemberDone    MemberStatus = "done"
	    MemberFailed  MemberStatus = "failed"
	)

<a name="MemoryContext"></a>
## type MemoryContext

MemoryContext wraps memory results with conversation metadata.

	type MemoryContext struct {
	    Results      []memory.MemoryResult `json:"results"`
	    LastIntent   *Intent               `json:"last_intent,omitempty"`
	    LastAgent    string                `json:"last_agent,omitempty"`
	    IntentCounts map[string]int        `json:"intent_counts,omitempty"`
	}

<a name="MemoryRecallMode"></a>
## type MemoryRecallMode

MemoryRecallMode determines how memory context is retrieved for an agent.

	type MemoryRecallMode string

<a name="RecallModeAuto"></a>

	const (
	    RecallModeAuto     MemoryRecallMode = "auto"     // Always auto-inject context before LLM calls
	    RecallModeOnQuery  MemoryRecallMode = "on-query" // Only fetch when agent calls memory_search tool
	    RecallModeHybrid   MemoryRecallMode = "hybrid"   // Auto-inject + tools available
	    RecallModeDisabled MemoryRecallMode = "disabled" // No memory injection, tools still available
	)

<a name="MessageClassification"></a>
## type MessageClassification

MessageClassification classifies the semantic type of a message for importance\-based retention.

	type MessageClassification int

<a name="MessageUnknown"></a>

	const (
	    // MessageUnknown is the default for unclassified messages.
	    MessageUnknown MessageClassification = iota
	    // MessageUserInput is the original user request or follow-up questions.
	    MessageUserInput
	    // MessageAssistantPlan is assistant output containing plans, task decomposition, or step-by-step thinking.
	    MessageAssistantPlan
	    // MessageAssistantConclusion is assistant output with final answers, summaries, or conclusions.
	    MessageAssistantConclusion
	    // MessageToolResult is the output from tool execution.
	    MessageToolResult
	    // MessageToolResultKey is a tool result containing key findings (e.g., file contents, search results).
	    MessageToolResultKey
	    // MessageReasoningStep is intermediate reasoning or exploration (lowest priority).
	    MessageReasoningStep
	)

<a name="MessageEndData"></a>
## type MessageEndData

MessageEndData is emitted when a message is finalized.

	type MessageEndData struct {
	    Role       string `json:"role"`
	    Content    string `json:"content"`
	    TokenCount int    `json:"token_count"`
	    ToolCalls  int    `json:"tool_calls"`
	}

<a name="MessageImportance"></a>
## type MessageImportance

MessageImportance is the priority level for message retention during truncation. Higher values = more important = retained longer.

	type MessageImportance int

<a name="ImportanceLow"></a>

	const (
	    ImportanceLow MessageImportance = iota
	    ImportanceMedium
	    ImportanceHigh
	    ImportanceCritical
	)

<a name="MessageQueue"></a>
## type MessageQueue

MessageQueue is a thread\-safe dual\-queue for steering and follow\-up messages.

	type MessageQueue struct {
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

<a name="NewMessageQueue"></a>
### func NewMessageQueue

	func NewMessageQueue(opts ...MessageQueueOption) *MessageQueue

NewMessageQueue creates a new MessageQueue.

<a name="MessageQueue.ClearPersister"></a>
### func \(\*MessageQueue\) ClearPersister

	func (q *MessageQueue) ClearPersister()

ClearPersister removes the persistence backend. Called after a conversation's RunOnce\(\) completes.

<a name="MessageQueue.Close"></a>
### func \(\*MessageQueue\) Close

	func (q *MessageQueue) Close()

Close marks the queue as closed. It persists pending follow\-ups if configured.

<a name="MessageQueue.DrainFollowUp"></a>
### func \(\*MessageQueue\) DrainFollowUp

	func (q *MessageQueue) DrainFollowUp() []QueuedMessage

DrainFollowUp returns follow\-up messages according to the DrainMode. DrainOne: returns at most one message. DrainAll: returns all pending messages.

<a name="MessageQueue.DrainSteering"></a>
### func \(\*MessageQueue\) DrainSteering

	func (q *MessageQueue) DrainSteering() []QueuedMessage

DrainSteering returns at most one steering message \(FIFO within the single\-slot queue\). Returns an empty slice if there are no steering messages.

<a name="MessageQueue.FollowUp"></a>
### func \(\*MessageQueue\) FollowUp

	func (q *MessageQueue) FollowUp(ctx context.Context, content, source string) error

FollowUp injects a follow\-up message into the follow\-up queue. Returns ErrQueueClosed if the queue is closed, ErrQueueFull if at capacity.

<a name="MessageQueue.GetGeneration"></a>
### func \(\*MessageQueue\) GetGeneration

	func (q *MessageQueue) GetGeneration() uint64

GetGeneration returns the current generation counter.

<a name="MessageQueue.HasFollowUp"></a>
### func \(\*MessageQueue\) HasFollowUp

	func (q *MessageQueue) HasFollowUp() bool

HasFollowUp returns true if the follow\-up queue has pending messages.

<a name="MessageQueue.HasSteering"></a>
### func \(\*MessageQueue\) HasSteering

	func (q *MessageQueue) HasSteering() bool

HasSteering returns true if the steering queue has pending messages.

<a name="MessageQueue.IsClosed"></a>
### func \(\*MessageQueue\) IsClosed

	func (q *MessageQueue) IsClosed() bool

IsClosed returns true if the queue has been closed.

<a name="MessageQueue.SetPersister"></a>
### func \(\*MessageQueue\) SetPersister

	func (q *MessageQueue) SetPersister(p QueuePersisterOps)

SetPersister attaches a persistence backend for follow\-up messages. Called per\-conversation in RunOnce\(\) when persistence is enabled.

<a name="MessageQueue.Status"></a>
### func \(\*MessageQueue\) Status

	func (q *MessageQueue) Status() QueueStatus

Status returns a snapshot of the queue's current state.

<a name="MessageQueue.Steer"></a>
### func \(\*MessageQueue\) Steer

	func (q *MessageQueue) Steer(ctx context.Context, content, source string) error

Steer injects a steering message into the steering queue. If a steering message already exists, it is replaced \(latest wins\). Returns ErrQueueClosed if the queue is closed, ErrQueueFull if at capacity.

<a name="ModelReassignmentDirective"></a>
## type ModelReassignmentDirective

ModelReassignmentDirective captures a user's model reassignment instruction.

	type ModelReassignmentDirective struct {
	    // Instruction is the raw user instruction text (e.g., "use GLM models for coding")
	    Instruction string `json:"instruction"`
	
	    // TargetScope - which intent type this applies to
	    // Examples: "synthesis"→IntentPlan, "coding"→IntentCode, "research"→IntentResearch
	    TargetScope string `json:"target_scope,omitempty"`
	
	    // TargetIntent - resolved intent type from scope keyword
	    TargetIntent *IntentType `json:"target_intent,omitempty"`
	
	    // ModelReferences - one or more model specs from user input
	    // Can be: "zai/glm-4.7", "glm-*", "provider:zai", "opus"
	    ModelReferences []string `json:"model_references"`
	
	    // ResolvedModels - after resolver processes references
	    ResolvedModels []*llm.ModelConfig `json:"resolved_models,omitempty"`
	
	    // ClarificationNeeded - set true if instruction is ambiguous
	    ClarificationNeeded bool `json:"clarification_needed,omitempty"`
	
	    // ClarificationQuestions - questions to ask user if ambiguous
	    ClarificationQuestions []string `json:"clarification_questions,omitempty"`
	}

<a name="MultiIntent"></a>
## type MultiIntent

MultiIntent represents multiple detected intents in a single request.

	type MultiIntent struct {
	    Intents      []*Intent `json:"intents"`
	    IsCompound   bool      `json:"is_compound"`
	    CompoundType string    `json:"compound_type,omitempty"` // "sequential" or "parallel"
	    Summary      string    `json:"summary"`
	}

<a name="MultiIntent.DetectCompound"></a>
### func \(\*MultiIntent\) DetectCompound

	func (m *MultiIntent) DetectCompound() bool

DetectCompound analyzes intents and determines if they're compound. Adds confidence and complexity guards \(Issues 0006, 0029\): \- Requires at least 2 intents with confidence \>= 0.5 \- Both must be non\-chat intents to qualify as compound

<a name="OrchestratorDeps"></a>
## type OrchestratorDeps

OrchestratorDeps holds dependencies for the orchestrator.

	type OrchestratorDeps struct {
	    Strategic           *StrategicPlanner
	    Tactical            *TacticalScheduler
	    PairManager         *PairManager
	    BusPairOrchestrator *PairOrchestrator    // optional: enables channel-based pairing (Option C)
	    PlanManager         *plan.PlanManager    // optional: plan system integration
	    CollaborationEngine *CollaborationEngine // optional: enables agent collaboration modes

	    Result   *ExecutionResult // replacement result (if Override=true)
