	}

<a name="PPConversation"></a>
## type PPConversation

PPConversation holds the shared state for a pair programming session.

	type PPConversation struct {
	    SessionID   string
	    Workspace   string
	    LastDiff    string
	    TurnManager *TurnManager
	    Converged   bool
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

<a name="PairContext"></a>
## type PairContext

PairContext holds the shared working memory for a pair session. Both actor and reviewer see the full context on each round.

	type PairContext struct {
	    // OriginalSpec is the initial task description / spec.
	    OriginalSpec string `json:"original_spec"`
	
	    // Attempts is the ordered history of all actor->reviewer rounds.
	    Attempts []*Attempt `json:"attempts"`
	
	    // AcceptedCriteria accumulates spec items that were satisfied in
	    // prior rounds. When this covers all criteria from the original
	    // spec, the session has converged.
	    AcceptedCriteria []string `json:"accepted_criteria"`
	
	    // PendingCriteria are spec items not yet satisfied.
	    PendingCriteria []string `json:"pending_criteria"`
	}

<a name="PairContext.ActorPrompt"></a>
### func \(\*PairContext\) ActorPrompt

	func (pc *PairContext) ActorPrompt() string

ActorPrompt builds the full prompt for the actor on a given round. It includes the original spec, all prior attempts and their reviews, and the remaining pending criteria.

<a name="PairContext.HasConverged"></a>
### func \(\*PairContext\) HasConverged

	func (pc *PairContext) HasConverged() bool

HasConverged returns true when no pending criteria remain.

<a name="PairContext.RecordAttempt"></a>
### func \(\*PairContext\) RecordAttempt

	func (pc *PairContext) RecordAttempt(attempt *Attempt)

RecordAttempt adds a completed attempt and updates criteria.

<a name="PairContext.ReviewerPrompt"></a>
### func \(\*PairContext\) ReviewerPrompt

	func (pc *PairContext) ReviewerPrompt(actorOutput string) string

ReviewerPrompt builds the full prompt for the reviewer on a given round. It includes the original spec, the actor's latest output, and the accumulated accepted/pending criteria.

<a name="PairManager"></a>
## type PairManager

PairManager drives the multi\-round actor\-\>reviewer loop for pair sessions. It holds active sessions in memory and publishes bus events on state changes.

	type PairManager struct {
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

<a name="NewPairManager"></a>
### func NewPairManager

	func NewPairManager(cfg PairManagerConfig) *PairManager

NewPairManager creates a new pair manager.

<a name="PairManager.ActiveSessionCount"></a>
### func \(\*PairManager\) ActiveSessionCount

	func (pm *PairManager) ActiveSessionCount() int

ActiveSessionCount returns the number of active \(non\-terminal\) sessions.

<a name="PairManager.CreateSession"></a>
### func \(\*PairManager\) CreateSession

	func (pm *PairManager) CreateSession(taskID, spec, actorID, reviewerID string, maxRounds int) *PairSession

CreateSession creates a new pair session for a task and registers it.

<a name="PairManager.GetSession"></a>
### func \(\*PairManager\) GetSession

	func (pm *PairManager) GetSession(sessionID string) (*PairSession, bool)

GetSession returns a pair session by ID.

<a name="PairManager.GetSessionByStep"></a>
### func \(\*PairManager\) GetSessionByStep

	func (pm *PairManager) GetSessionByStep(stepID string) (*PairSession, bool)

GetSessionByStep returns the pair session that owns the given step, if any.

<a name="PairManager.GetSessionByTask"></a>
### func \(\*PairManager\) GetSessionByTask

	func (pm *PairManager) GetSessionByTask(taskID string) (*PairSession, bool)

GetSessionByTask returns the active pair session for a task, if any.

<a name="PairManager.ListSessions"></a>
### func \(\*PairManager\) ListSessions

	func (pm *PairManager) ListSessions(activeOnly bool) []*PairSession

ListSessions returns all sessions, optionally filtered by state.

<a name="PairManager.RemoveSession"></a>
### func \(\*PairManager\) RemoveSession

	func (pm *PairManager) RemoveSession(sessionID string)

RemoveSession removes a completed session from the manager.

<a name="PairManager.RunAllRounds"></a>
### func \(\*PairManager\) RunAllRounds

	func (pm *PairManager) RunAllRounds(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) (*PairSession, error)

RunAllRounds runs the full loop until convergence, exhaustion, or error.

<a name="PairManager.RunRound"></a>
### func \(\*PairManager\) RunRound

	func (pm *PairManager) RunRound(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) (*Attempt, error)

RunRound executes one actor\-\>reviewer round for the given session. It runs the actor agent, then the reviewer agent, and records the attempt. Returns the attempt and an error if the round could not complete.

<a name="PairManagerConfig"></a>
## type PairManagerConfig

PairManagerConfig holds configuration for creating a PairManager.

	type PairManagerConfig struct {
	    Registry  *AgentRegistry
	    TaskStore *task.Store
	    StepStore *task.StepStore
	    Bus       *bus.MessageBus
	    Logger    *slog.Logger
	}

<a name="PairModality"></a>
## type PairModality

PairModality defines the type of agentic pairing applied to a task step. The orchestrator selects a modality based on task complexity, step tool hint, and user preferences. This enum is shared across all agentic pair options.

	type PairModality int

<a name="PairModalityNone"></a>

	const (
	    // PairModalityNone means no agentic pairing; single-agent execution.
	    PairModalityNone PairModality = iota // none
	    // PairModalitySpecReview is Option A: specification-driven review loop
	    // where acceptance criteria are generated during planning and the reviewer
	    // checks against them.
	    PairModalitySpecReview // spec_review
	    // PairModalityPairSession is Option B: shared-context pair session where
	    // two agents iterate on a full task with accumulated review history.
	    PairModalityPairSession // pair_session
	    // PairModalityDebate is Option C: bus-channel-based dual-agent conversation
	    // where agents take turns via a shared topic.
	    PairModalityDebate // debate
	    // PairModalityInline is Option D: tool-based inline review where the actor
	    // calls request_review within its own execution loop.
	    PairModalityInline // inline
	)

<a name="ParsePairModality"></a>
### func ParsePairModality

	func ParsePairModality(s string) PairModality

ParsePairModality parses a string into a PairModality, returning PairModalityNone for unrecognized values.

<a name="PairModality.IsActive"></a>
### func \(PairModality\) IsActive

	func (m PairModality) IsActive() bool

IsActive returns true if the modality represents an active pairing \(not none\).

<a name="PairModality.MarshalJSON"></a>
### func \(PairModality\) MarshalJSON

	func (m PairModality) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error)

MarshalJSON implements json.Marshaler for PairModality.

<a name="PairModality.String"></a>
### func \(PairModality\) String

	func (i PairModality) String() string



<a name="PairModality.UnmarshalJSON"></a>
### func \(\*PairModality\) UnmarshalJSON

	func (m *PairModality) UnmarshalJSON(data []byte) error

UnmarshalJSON implements json.Unmarshaler for PairModality.

<a name="PairResult"></a>
## type PairResult

PairResult is the final result of a completed pair session.

	type PairResult struct {
	    SessionID    string      `json:"session_id"`
	    FinalOutput  string      `json:"final_output"`
	    Turns        []PairTurn  `json:"turns"`
	    TotalTurns   int         `json:"total_turns"`
	    FinalVerdict PairVerdict `json:"final_verdict"`
	}

<a name="PairSession"></a>
## type PairSession

PairSession ties two agents together for a multi\-round task with shared context.

	type PairSession struct {
	    // ID is the unique session identifier.
	    ID  string `json:"id"`
	
	    // TaskID is the parent task this session serves.
	    TaskID string `json:"task_id"`
	
	    // ActorAgentID is the agent that executes the work (e.g., "coder").
	    ActorAgentID string `json:"actor_agent_id"`
	
	    // ReviewerAgentID is the agent that reviews the work (e.g., "planner" or "analyst").
	    ReviewerAgentID string `json:"reviewer_agent_id"`
	
	    // Context holds the shared working memory.
	    Context PairContext `json:"context"`
	
	    // MaxRounds is the maximum number of actor->reviewer rounds before giving up.
	    MaxRounds int `json:"max_rounds"`
	
	    // State is the current lifecycle state.
	    State PairSessionState `json:"state"`
	
	    // StepIDs tracks all step IDs created for this pair session, in order.
	    // Used by the tactical scheduler to skip pair-managed steps.
	    StepIDs []string `json:"step_ids,omitempty"`
	
	    // CreatedAt is when the session was created.
	    CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
	
	    // UpdatedAt is when the session was last modified.
	    UpdatedAt time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
	}

<a name="NewPairSession"></a>
### func NewPairSession

	func NewPairSession(taskID, originalSpec, actorID, reviewerID string, maxRounds int) *PairSession

NewPairSession creates a new pair session for a task.

<a name="PairSession.AddStepID"></a>
### func \(\*PairSession\) AddStepID

	func (ps *PairSession) AddStepID(stepID string)

AddStepID records a step ID as belonging to this pair session.

<a name="PairSession.CurrentRound"></a>
### func \(\*PairSession\) CurrentRound

	func (ps *PairSession) CurrentRound() int

CurrentRound returns the 1\-indexed round number \(number of attempts \+ 1\).

<a name="PairSession.IsExhausted"></a>
### func \(\*PairSession\) IsExhausted

	func (ps *PairSession) IsExhausted() bool

IsExhausted returns true if the session has used all its rounds.

<a name="PairSession.MarkConverged"></a>
### func \(\*PairSession\) MarkConverged

	func (ps *PairSession) MarkConverged()

MarkConverged transitions the session to the converged state.

<a name="PairSession.MarkExhausted"></a>
### func \(\*PairSession\) MarkExhausted

	func (ps *PairSession) MarkExhausted()

MarkExhausted transitions the session to the exhausted state.

<a name="PairSession.MarkFailed"></a>
### func \(\*PairSession\) MarkFailed

	func (ps *PairSession) MarkFailed()

MarkFailed transitions the session to the failed state.

<a name="PairSession.OwnsStep"></a>

OwnsStep returns true if the given step ID belongs to this pair session.

<a name="PairSession.OwnsTask"></a>

<a name="PairSessionState"></a>
## type PairSessionState

PairSessionState represents the lifecycle state of a pair session.

	type PairSessionState string

<a name="PairSessionActive"></a>

	const (
	    PairSessionActive    PairSessionState = "active"
	    PairSessionConverged PairSessionState = "converged"
	    PairSessionExhausted PairSessionState = "exhausted" // max rounds reached
	    PairSessionFailed    PairSessionState = "failed"
	    PairSessionCancelled PairSessionState = "cancelled"
	)

<a name="PairSessionState.IsTerminal"></a>
### func \(PairSessionState\) IsTerminal

	func (s PairSessionState) IsTerminal() bool

IsTerminal returns true if the pair session is in a terminal state.

<a name="PairStartRequest"></a>
## type PairStartRequest

PairStartRequest initiates a new pair conversation session.

	type PairStartRequest struct {
	    // SessionID is the unique pair session identifier.
	    SessionID string `json:"session_id"`
	    // ActorID is the agent that performs the initial work.
	    ActorID string `json:"actor_id"`
	    // ReviewerID is the agent that reviews the actor's output.
	    ReviewerID string `json:"reviewer_id"`
	    // InitialPrompt is the user's original request.
	    InitialPrompt string `json:"initial_prompt"`
	    // MaxTurns is the maximum number of actor-reviewer cycles (0 = unlimited, default 5).
	    MaxTurns int `json:"max_turns,omitempty"`
	    // Metadata holds optional extra data from the dispatcher.
	    Metadata map[string]string `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
	}

<a name="PairTurn"></a>
## type PairTurn

PairTurn represents a single turn in the pair conversation.

	type PairTurn struct {
	    // SessionID identifies the pair session.
	    SessionID string `json:"session_id"`
	    // TurnNumber is the sequential turn index (0-based).
	    TurnNumber int `json:"turn_number"`
	    // AgentID is the agent that produced this turn.
	    AgentID string `json:"agent_id"`
	    // Role is either "actor" or "reviewer".
	    Role string `json:"role"`
	    // Content is the agent's output text.
	    Content string `json:"content"`
	    // Verdict is the reviewer's classification (empty for actor turns).
	    Verdict PairVerdict `json:"verdict,omitempty"`
	    // Feedback is the reviewer's feedback (empty for approved or actor turns).
	    Feedback string `json:"feedback,omitempty"`
	}

<a name="PairVerdict"></a>
## type PairVerdict

PairVerdict represents the outcome of a reviewer's evaluation.

	type PairVerdict string

<a name="PairVerdictApproved"></a>

	const (
	    PairVerdictApproved  PairVerdict = "approved"
	    PairVerdictRejected  PairVerdict = "rejected"
	    PairVerdictNeedsMore PairVerdict = "needs_more"
	)

<a name="ParallelTeamDriver"></a>
## type ParallelTeamDriver

ParallelTeamDriver implements CollaborationMode for N\-agent parallel team execution with a lead agent that synthesizes partial results.

	type ParallelTeamDriver struct {
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

<a name="NewParallelTeamDriver"></a>
### func NewParallelTeamDriver

	func NewParallelTeamDriver(deps ParallelTeamDriverDeps) *ParallelTeamDriver

NewParallelTeamDriver creates a new parallel team driver.

<a name="ParallelTeamDriver.CanInitiate"></a>
### func \(\*ParallelTeamDriver\) CanInitiate

	func (d *ParallelTeamDriver) CanInitiate(_ string, reason string) bool

CanInitiate returns true if the reason mentions team, multi, or parallel.

<a name="ParallelTeamDriver.Name"></a>
### func \(\*ParallelTeamDriver\) Name

	func (d *ParallelTeamDriver) Name() string

Name returns the mode name.

<a name="ParallelTeamDriver.Run"></a>
### func \(\*ParallelTeamDriver\) Run

	func (d *ParallelTeamDriver) Run(ctx context.Context, sess *CollaborationSession) (*CollaborationResult, error)

Run executes the parallel team collaboration pipeline:

1. Parse team configuration from session metadata
2. Fan out subtasks to roster agents concurrently
3. Collect results via errgroup
4. Lead agent synthesizes final result from partial results
5. Return CollaborationResult with aggregated output

	}

<a name="PlanRequest"></a>

	type PlanRequest struct {
	    TaskID    string `json:"task_id"`
	    SessionID string `json:"session_id"`
	    Input     string `json:"input"`
	    Intent    string `json:"intent"`
	
	    // Compound support (Phase 3)
	    IsCompound   bool   `json:"is_compound,omitempty"`
	    CompoundType string `json:"compound_type,omitempty"`
	
	    // PlanningContext carries verified context from a Prometheus-style interview.

	    // TrueAnalysis carries IntentGate-style pre-classification analysis.
	    TrueAnalysis *TrueIntentAnalysis `json:"true_analysis,omitempty"`
	}

<a name="QueueConfig"></a>
## type QueueConfig

QueueConfig holds configuration for both steering and follow\-up queues.

	type QueueConfig struct {
	    // Steering drain mode - always DrainOne, stored for API consistency.
	    SteeringDrain DrainMode `json:"steering_drain"`
	    // FollowUp drain mode: "one" returns a single message, "all" returns all pending.
	    FollowUpDrain DrainMode `json:"follow_up_drain"`
	    // MaxSteering is the maximum number of steering messages (enforced: 1).
	    MaxSteering int `json:"max_steering"`
	    // MaxFollowUp is the maximum number of follow-up messages allowed.
	    MaxFollowUp int `json:"max_follow_up"`
	    // PersistFollowUp controls whether pending follow-ups are persisted on close.
	    PersistFollowUp bool `json:"persist_follow_up"`
	    // FlushDelayMs is the write-behind flush delay in milliseconds.
	    FlushDelayMs int `json:"flush_delay_ms"`
	}

<a name="DefaultQueueConfig"></a>
### func DefaultQueueConfig

	func DefaultQueueConfig() QueueConfig

DefaultQueueConfig returns a QueueConfig with sensible defaults.

<a name="QueueEntry"></a>
## type QueueEntry

QueueEntry wraps a MessageQueue with generation tracking.

	type QueueEntry struct {
	    Queue      *MessageQueue
	    Generation uint64
	}

<a name="QueueEventPayload"></a>
## type QueueEventPayload

QueueEventPayload is attached to bus events when messages are added.

	type QueueEventPayload struct {
	    ConversationID string `json:"conversation_id"`
	    QueueType      string `json:"queue_type"`
	    MessageID      string `json:"message_id"`
	    ContentPreview string `json:"content_preview"`
	    Source         string `json:"source"`
	    QueueDepth     int    `json:"queue_depth"`
	}

<a name="QueueInjectedPayload"></a>
## type QueueInjectedPayload

QueueInjectedPayload is attached to bus events when the agent loop injects queued messages into a conversation.

	type QueueInjectedPayload struct {
	    ConversationID string    `json:"conversation_id"`
	    QueueType      QueueType `json:"queue_type"`
	    Count          int       `json:"count"`
	    MessageIDs     []string  `json:"message_ids"`
	    Iteration      int       `json:"iteration"`
	}

<a name="QueuePersister"></a>
## type QueuePersister

QueuePersister implements write\-behind persistence for follow\-up messages.

	type QueuePersister struct {
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

<a name="NewQueuePersister"></a>
### func NewQueuePersister

	func NewQueuePersister(db *sql.DB, conversationID string, logger *slog.Logger) (*QueuePersister, error)

NewQueuePersister creates a new write\-behind persister. It initializes the database schema immediately.

<a name="QueuePersister.ClearPending"></a>
### func \(\*QueuePersister\) ClearPending

	func (p *QueuePersister) ClearPending() error

ClearPending removes all persisted follow\-ups for this conversation.

<a name="QueuePersister.EnqueueAsync"></a>
### func \(\*QueuePersister\) EnqueueAsync

	func (p *QueuePersister) EnqueueAsync(msg QueuedMessage)

EnqueueAsync buffers a message for later flushing. It starts or resets the flush timer \(debounced write\-behind\). If the pending buffer reaches MaxPending, an immediate flush is triggered first.

<a name="QueuePersister.Flush"></a>
### func \(\*QueuePersister\) Flush

	func (p *QueuePersister) Flush()

Flush writes all pending buffered messages to SQLite.

<a name="QueuePersister.LoadPending"></a>
### func \(\*QueuePersister\) LoadPending

	func (p *QueuePersister) LoadPending() ([]QueuedMessage, error)

LoadPending returns all persisted follow\-ups for this conversation.

<a name="QueuePersister.PersistSync"></a>
### func \(\*QueuePersister\) PersistSync

	func (p *QueuePersister) PersistSync(msg QueuedMessage) error

PersistSync immediately inserts a message into SQLite.

<a name="QueuePersister.Stop"></a>
### func \(\*QueuePersister\) Stop

	func (p *QueuePersister) Stop()

Stop halts the flush timer and flushes any remaining pending messages.

<a name="QueuePersister.WithBus"></a>
### func \(\*QueuePersister\) WithBus

	func (p *QueuePersister) WithBus(b *bus.MessageBus)

WithBus attaches a message bus for event publishing on persist.

<a name="QueuePersisterConfig"></a>
## type QueuePersisterConfig

QueuePersisterConfig holds configuration for QueuePersister.

	type QueuePersisterConfig struct {
	    FlushDelay time.Duration
	    MaxPending int
	}

<a name="DefaultQueuePersisterConfig"></a>
### func DefaultQueuePersisterConfig

	func DefaultQueuePersisterConfig() QueuePersisterConfig

DefaultQueuePersisterConfig returns defaults.

<a name="QueuePersisterOps"></a>

	type QueuePersisterOps interface {
	    PersistSync(msg QueuedMessage) error
	}

<a name="RegistryConfig"></a>
## type RegistryConfig

RegistryConfig holds configuration for creating an AgentRegistry.

	type RegistryConfig struct {
	    MemvidClient    *memvid.Client
	    TaskStore       *task.Store
	    LLMClient       *llm.Client
	    Resolver        *llm.Resolver
	    MessageBus      *bus.MessageBus
	    SecurityChecker *security.PermissionChecker
	    ToolRegistry    ToolRegistry
	    ShadowManager   *shadow.Manager
	    Logger          *slog.Logger
	
	    // Agent validation components (shared across all agent loops)
	    Watchdog              *Watchdog
	    HallucinationDetector *HallucinationDetector
	    ArtifactManager       *ArtifactManager
	
	    // TT-SR stream rule enforcement (shared across all agent loops)
	    TTSRManager *TTSRManager
	
	    // BundledAgentsPath is the path to bundled AGENT.md files (e.g., "config/agents").
	    BundledAgentsPath string
	
	    // GlobalRules is the global rules content to inject into all agents.
	    // If empty, the registry will auto-discover rules using RulesDiscovery.
	    GlobalRules string
	
	    // Queues holds steering and follow-up message queue settings from config.
	    // When non-zero, these values override the code defaults.
	    Queues config.AgentQueuesConfig
	
	    // DB is an optional SQLite connection used for queue persistence.
	    // When set, follow-up messages are persisted to the queued_followups table.
	    DB  *sql.DB
	}

<a name="ReportCapture"></a>

<a name="ReportRouter"></a>
## type ReportRouter

ReportRouter executes routing decisions after an agent completes. It replaces the dead\-end DetermineRouteAction call in the dispatcher.

	type ReportRouter struct {
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

<a name="NewReportRouter"></a>
### func NewReportRouter

	func NewReportRouter(cfg ReportRouterConfig) *ReportRouter

NewReportRouter creates a new report router.

<a name="ReportRouter.Route"></a>
### func \(\*ReportRouter\) Route

	func (r *ReportRouter) Route(ctx context.Context, params RouteParams) RouteResult

Route determines what to do after an agent completes its work. For RouteActionClose, it returns the display response. For RouteActionRoute, it would run the next agent \(handled by the dispatcher loop\). For RouteActionNotifyUser/RouteActionNotifyError, it flags that user input is needed. At max depth, all actions become RouteActionNotifyUser.

<a name="ReportRouterConfig"></a>
## type ReportRouterConfig

ReportRouterConfig configures the report router.

	type ReportRouterConfig struct {
	    Registry   *AgentRegistry
	    Dispatcher *Dispatcher
	    Bus        interface{ Publish(string, any) }
	    Logger     *slog.Logger
	    MaxDepth   int
	}

<a name="ResourcesUpdateData"></a>
## type ResourcesUpdateData

ResourcesUpdateData is emitted when resource usage changes.

	type ResourcesUpdateData struct {
	    TokensUsed     int `json:"tokens_used"`
	    TokensBudget   int `json:"tokens_budget"`
	    IterationsUsed int `json:"iterations_used"`
	    IterationsMax  int `json:"iterations_max"`
	}

<a name="ResultCache"></a>
## type ResultCache

ResultCache provides an in\-memory cache for tool execution results.

	type ResultCache struct {
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

<a name="NewResultCache"></a>
### func NewResultCache

	func NewResultCache(config CacheConfig, logger *slog.Logger) *ResultCache

NewResultCache creates a new result cache with the given configuration. If config.DefaultTTL is 0, defaults to 5 minutes. If config.MaxEntries is 0, defaults to 1000. If config.CleanupFreq is 0, defaults to 1 minute. If config.EnabledTools is nil/empty, uses default cached tools.

<a name="ResultCache.Clear"></a>
### func \(\*ResultCache\) Clear

	func (c *ResultCache) Clear()

Clear removes all entries from the cache and resets statistics.

<a name="ResultCache.Get"></a>
### func \(\*ResultCache\) Get

	func (c *ResultCache) Get(toolName string, args map[string]any) (any, bool)

Get retrieves a cached result for the given tool and arguments. Returns the cached result and true if found \(and not expired\). Returns nil and false if not found, expired, or tool not cacheable.

<a name="ResultCache.Invalidate"></a>
### func \(\*ResultCache\) Invalidate

	func (c *ResultCache) Invalidate(toolName string, args map[string]any)

Invalidate removes a specific cached entry. Does nothing if the entry doesn't exist.

<a name="ResultCache.InvalidateTool"></a>
### func \(\*ResultCache\) InvalidateTool

	func (c *ResultCache) InvalidateTool(toolName string)

InvalidateTool removes all cached entries for a specific tool.

<a name="ResultCache.Put"></a>
### func \(\*ResultCache\) Put

	func (c *ResultCache) Put(toolName string, args map[string]any, result any)

Put stores a tool execution result in the cache. Does nothing if the tool is not enabled for caching.

<a name="ResultCache.Start"></a>
### func \(\*ResultCache\) Start

	func (c *ResultCache) Start()

Start starts the background cleanup goroutine.

<a name="ResultCache.Stats"></a>
### func \(\*ResultCache\) Stats

	func (c *ResultCache) Stats() map[string]any

Stats returns current cache statistics.

<a name="ResultCache.Stop"></a>
### func \(\*ResultCache\) Stop

	func (c *ResultCache) Stop()

Stop stops the background cleanup goroutine.

<a name="ResultPayload"></a>
## type ResultPayload

ResultPayload is the payload for "result" type messages.

	type ResultPayload struct {
	    // Summary is a brief description of the outcome.
	    Summary string `json:"summary"`
	
	    // Status is the completion status.
	    Status string `json:"status"` // "completed", "partial", "failed"
	
	    // CreatedMemories are memory IDs created during execution.
	    CreatedMemories []string `json:"created_memories,omitempty"`
	
	    // Artifacts are file paths, URLs, or other outputs.
	    Artifacts []string `json:"artifacts,omitempty"`
	
	    // NextSteps are suggested follow-up actions.
	    NextSteps []string `json:"next_steps,omitempty"`
	
	    // Error contains error details if status is "failed".
	    Error string `json:"error,omitempty"`
	}

	}

	func (rm *ReviewManager) GetValidationPolicy() *ValidationPolicy

GetValidationPolicy returns the current validation policy.

<a name="ReviewManager.HandleReviewResult"></a>
### func \(\*ReviewManager\) HandleReviewResult

	func (rm *ReviewManager) HandleReviewResult(ctx context.Context, stepID string, result *ReviewResult, spec *TaskSpec) ([]*task.TaskStep, error)

HandleReviewResult processes a review result and updates step state. Returns any newly\-created revision step\(s\) so that callers with scheduling responsibilities \(e.g. TacticalScheduler\) can enqueue them.

	func (rm *ReviewManager) SetValidationPolicy(policy *ValidationPolicy)

SetValidationPolicy updates the validation policy.

<a name="ReviewManager.ValidateCompletion"></a>
### func \(\*ReviewManager\) ValidateCompletion

	func (rm *ReviewManager) ValidateCompletion(ctx context.Context, step *task.TaskStep, taskDesc string) (*ValidationResult, error)

ValidateCompletion checks that all assigned work for a step is actually done. It examines the step's result, evidence, and claims to determine if the work described in the step was fully completed.

<a name="ReviewManagerConfig"></a>
## type ReviewManagerConfig

ReviewManagerConfig holds configuration for creating a ReviewManager.

	type ReviewManagerConfig struct {
	    Registry         *AgentRegistry
	    StepStore        *task.StepStore
	    TaskStore        *task.Store
	    Policy           *ReviewPolicy
	    ValidationPolicy *ValidationPolicy
	    Bus              *bus.MessageBus
	    Logger           *slog.Logger
	}

<a name="ReviewPolicy"></a>
## type ReviewPolicy

ReviewPolicy determines which steps require review and how.

	type ReviewPolicy struct {
	    // Tool hints that ALWAYS require review
	    RequireReview []string
	
	    // Tool hints that NEVER require review (trusted operations)
	    SkipReview []string
	
	    // Agent-specific reviewer mappings
	    // e.g., coder → code-reviewer, debugger → debug-reviewer
	    ReviewerMapping map[string]string
	
	    // Maximum revision cycles before requiring human intervention
	    MaxRevisionCycles int
	
	    // File patterns that auto-approve (low-risk changes)
	    AutoApprovePatterns []string
	
	    // Whether review is enabled globally
	    Enabled bool
	}

<a name="DefaultReviewPolicy"></a>
### func DefaultReviewPolicy

	func DefaultReviewPolicy() *ReviewPolicy

DefaultReviewPolicy returns sensible defaults for review policy.

<a name="ReviewPolicy.ExceedsMaxRevisions"></a>
### func \(\*ReviewPolicy\) ExceedsMaxRevisions

	func (p *ReviewPolicy) ExceedsMaxRevisions(step *task.TaskStep) bool

ExceedsMaxRevisions checks if a step has exceeded the maximum revision cycles.

<a name="ReviewPolicy.NeedsReview"></a>
### func \(\*ReviewPolicy\) NeedsReview

	func (p *ReviewPolicy) NeedsReview(step *task.TaskStep) bool

NeedsReview determines if a step requires review based on policy.

<a name="ReviewPolicy.RequiresHumanIntervention"></a>
### func \(\*ReviewPolicy\) RequiresHumanIntervention

	func (p *ReviewPolicy) RequiresHumanIntervention(step *task.TaskStep) bool

RequiresHumanIntervention returns true if human intervention is needed.

<a name="ReviewPolicy.SelectReviewer"></a>
### func \(\*ReviewPolicy\) SelectReviewer

	func (p *ReviewPolicy) SelectReviewer(step *task.TaskStep) string

SelectReviewer selects the appropriate reviewer agent for a step.

<a name="ReviewPolicy.ShouldAutoApprove"></a>

	func (p *ReviewPolicy) ShouldAutoApprove(step *task.TaskStep) bool

ShouldAutoApprove checks if a step should auto\-approve based on its description.

<a name="ReviewStatus"></a>
## type ReviewStatus

ReviewStatus represents the outcome of a review.

	type ReviewStatus string

<a name="ReviewApproved"></a>

	    ReviewApproved  ReviewStatus = "approved"
	    ReviewRejected  ReviewStatus = "rejected"
	    ReviewNeedsInfo ReviewStatus = "needs_info"
	)

<a name="RouteAction"></a>
## type RouteAction

RouteAction represents the action to take based on a report.

	type RouteAction int

<a name="RouteActionClose"></a>

	const (
	    // RouteActionClose closes the task and notifies the user.
	    RouteActionClose RouteAction = iota
	
	    // RouteActionRoute routes to the suggested next agent.
	    RouteActionRoute
	
	    // RouteActionNotifyUser notifies the user and awaits input.
	    RouteActionNotifyUser
	
	    // RouteActionNotifyError notifies the user of failure.
	    RouteActionNotifyError
	)

<a name="DetermineRouteAction"></a>
### func DetermineRouteAction

	func DetermineRouteAction(report *AgentReport) RouteAction

DetermineRouteAction determines what action to take based on a report. This implements the dispatcher feedback loop logic.

<a name="RouteAction.String"></a>
### func \(RouteAction\) String

	func (a RouteAction) String() string

String returns a human\-readable description of the route action.

<a name="SessionTracker.GetDominantIntent"></a>
### func \(\*SessionTracker\) GetDominantIntent

	func (t *SessionTracker) GetDominantIntent(sessionID string) string

GetDominantIntent returns the most frequent intent in session.

<a name="SessionTracker.GetIdleSessions"></a>
### func \(\*SessionTracker\) GetIdleSessions

	func (t *SessionTracker) GetIdleSessions(idleDuration time.Duration) []*TrackerSessionState

GetIdleSessions returns sessions that have been idle for the specified duration.

<a name="SessionTracker.GetIntentCounts"></a>
### func \(\*SessionTracker\) GetIntentCounts

	func (t *SessionTracker) GetIntentCounts(sessionID string) map[string]int

GetIntentCounts returns intent frequency counts.

<a name="SessionTracker.GetLastAgent"></a>
### func \(\*SessionTracker\) GetLastAgent

	func (t *SessionTracker) GetLastAgent(sessionID string) string

GetLastAgent returns the most recent agent type.

<a name="SessionTracker.GetLastIntent"></a>
### func \(\*SessionTracker\) GetLastIntent

	func (t *SessionTracker) GetLastIntent(sessionID string) *Intent

GetLastIntent returns a copy of the most recent intent.

<a name="SessionTracker.GetSession"></a>
### func \(\*SessionTracker\) GetSession

	func (t *SessionTracker) GetSession(sessionID string) *TrackerSessionState

GetSession returns session state \(read\-only lookup, no cleanup\).

<a name="SessionTracker.PersistIdleSessions"></a>
### func \(\*SessionTracker\) PersistIdleSessions

	func (t *SessionTracker) PersistIdleSessions(ctx context.Context) error

PersistIdleSessions persists sessions that have been idle for the configured threshold. Returns the last persistence error encountered, or nil if all sessions persisted successfully \(or were not idle / had already been persisted\). AGENT\-20 FIX: Previously returned nil even when persistSession failed. Now tracks the last error and returns it after the loop. Callsites that need per\-session error reporting should inspect the error return value.

<a name="SessionTracker.RecordIntent"></a>
### func \(\*SessionTracker\) RecordIntent

	func (t *SessionTracker) RecordIntent(sessionID string, intent *Intent, agentID string)

RecordIntent logs an intent for a session.

<a name="SessionTracker.RecordMetrics"></a>
### func \(\*SessionTracker\) RecordMetrics

	func (t *SessionTracker) RecordMetrics(sessionID string, metrics SessionMetrics)

RecordMetrics records session metrics.

<a name="SessionTracker.StartBackgroundPersistence"></a>
### func \(\*SessionTracker\) StartBackgroundPersistence

	func (t *SessionTracker) StartBackgroundPersistence(ctx context.Context)

StartBackgroundPersistence starts a background goroutine that persists idle sessions. The goroutine runs every hour and persists sessions idle for \> sessionIdleTriggerHours. Call StopBackgroundPersistence\(\) to stop the goroutine.

<a name="SessionTracker.StopBackgroundPersistence"></a>
### func \(\*SessionTracker\) StopBackgroundPersistence

	func (t *SessionTracker) StopBackgroundPersistence()

StopBackgroundPersistence stops the background persistence goroutine. Safe to call multiple times; only the first call has any effect.

	type SessionTrackerConfig struct {

	    SessionIdleTriggerHours int
	    MemvidClient            *memvid.Client
	}

<a name="StatusPayload"></a>
## type StatusPayload

StatusPayload is the payload for "status" type messages.

	type StatusPayload struct {
	    // Progress is completion percentage (0-100).
	    Progress float64 `json:"progress"`
	
	    // Phase is the current execution phase.
	    Phase string `json:"phase"`
	
	    // Message is a status message.
	    Message string `json:"message"`
	}

	    Required           bool   `json:"required"`
	}

	    Description          string   `json:"description"`
	    ToolHint             string   `json:"tool_hint,omitempty"`
	    MemoryRefs           []string `json:"memory_refs,omitempty"`
	    AccumulatedContext   string   `json:"accumulated_context,omitempty"`

	}

	type StopDecision struct {

	    Reason string // used in the "wrap up" message
	}

<a name="StrategicPlanner"></a>
## type StrategicPlanner

StrategicPlanner decomposes tasks into steps using an LLM planner agent.

	type StrategicPlanner struct {
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

<a name="NewStrategicPlanner"></a>
### func NewStrategicPlanner

	func NewStrategicPlanner(cfg StrategicPlannerConfig) *StrategicPlanner

NewStrategicPlanner creates a new strategic planner.

<a name="StrategicPlanner.ApprovePlan"></a>
### func \(\*StrategicPlanner\) ApprovePlan

	func (sp *StrategicPlanner) ApprovePlan(ctx context.Context, taskID string) error

ApprovePlan resumes a plan that was paused at the approval gate. It loads the persisted steps, sets UserApproved on the planning context, transitions to StateExecuting, and triggers scheduling.

<a name="StrategicPlanner.ConductInterview"></a>
### func \(\*StrategicPlanner\) ConductInterview

	func (sp *StrategicPlanner) ConductInterview(ctx context.Context, req PlanRequest) (*plan.PlanningContext, error)

ConductInterview determines whether an interview is needed for the given plan request. If the TrueAnalysis indicates high ambiguity or broad scope, it uses the LLM to generate targeted interview questions. If interview answers are already present, it marks the interview as completed and returns.

Returns a PlanningContext that may or may not have InterviewCompleted set to true. If the interview is incomplete, the caller should present the questions to the user and re\-invoke ConductInterview once answers are collected.

<a name="StrategicPlanner.Plan"></a>
### func \(\*StrategicPlanner\) Plan

	func (sp *StrategicPlanner) Plan(ctx context.Context, req PlanRequest) error

Plan decomposes a task into executable steps.

<a name="StrategicPlanner.RejectPlan"></a>
### func \(\*StrategicPlanner\) RejectPlan

	func (sp *StrategicPlanner) RejectPlan(ctx context.Context, taskID string, reason string) error

RejectPlan cancels a plan that was awaiting approval. It sets the task to StateRejected and publishes a rejection event.

<a name="StrategicPlanner.ReplanFailedTask"></a>
### func \(\*StrategicPlanner\) ReplanFailedTask

	func (sp *StrategicPlanner) ReplanFailedTask(ctx context.Context, taskID, failureReason string) error

ReplanFailedTask re\-plans a failed task into smaller steps for retry. This is called by the EscalationManager when a task fails and needs to be broken down into more manageable pieces.

	    Registry       *AgentRegistry
	    TaskStore      *task.Store
	    StepStore      *task.StepStore
	    Bus            *bus.MessageBus
	    Logger         *slog.Logger
	    PairManager    *PairManager
	    MaxPlanSteps   int
	    PlannerTimeout time.Duration
	}

<a name="SynthesizedProgressEvent"></a>

<a name="TTSRManager"></a>
## type TTSRManager

TTSRManager manages stream rule monitoring and enforcement.

	type TTSRManager struct {
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

<a name="NewTTSRManager"></a>
### func NewTTSRManager

	func NewTTSRManager(logger *slog.Logger) *TTSRManager

NewTTSRManager creates a new TT\-SR manager.

<a name="TTSRManager.CheckDelta"></a>
### func \(\*TTSRManager\) CheckDelta

	func (m *TTSRManager) CheckDelta(source string, delta string, turnNum int) []*TTSRRule

CheckDelta checks a complete response \(or streaming delta\) against all rules. source is "text", "thinking", or "tool\_call". Returns a slice of matched rules \(empty if no match\).

<a name="TTSRManager.HasRules"></a>
### func \(\*TTSRManager\) HasRules

	func (m *TTSRManager) HasRules() bool

HasRules returns true if the manager has any loaded rules.

<a name="TTSRManager.InjectionState"></a>
### func \(\*TTSRManager\) InjectionState

	func (m *TTSRManager) InjectionState() map[string]int

InjectionState returns the current injection state for persistence.

<a name="TTSRManager.LoadRules"></a>
### func \(\*TTSRManager\) LoadRules

	func (m *TTSRManager) LoadRules(skillsDir string) error

LoadRules scans a directory for skill/rule files with a scope field in their YAML frontmatter \(indicating a TT\-SR rule\). Each matching file is parsed and its compiled rules are added to the manager.

<a name="TTSRManager.LoadRulesFromDirs"></a>
### func \(\*TTSRManager\) LoadRulesFromDirs

	func (m *TTSRManager) LoadRulesFromDirs(dirs []string) error

LoadRulesFromDirs loads TT\-SR rules from multiple directories, appending rules from each. Existing rules are reset before loading. Non\-existent directories are silently skipped.

<a name="TTSRManager.MarkInjected"></a>
### func \(\*TTSRManager\) MarkInjected

	func (m *TTSRManager) MarkInjected(ruleName string, turnNum int)

MarkInjected marks a rule as having been injected at the given turn.

<a name="TTSRManager.RestoreInjected"></a>
### func \(\*TTSRManager\) RestoreInjected

	func (m *TTSRManager) RestoreInjected(injected map[string]int)

RestoreInjected restores injection state from persistence.

<a name="TTSRManager.Rules"></a>
### func \(\*TTSRManager\) Rules

	func (m *TTSRManager) Rules() []*TTSRRule

Rules returns a copy of the current rules list \(for inspection/testing\).

<a name="TTSRRule"></a>
## type TTSRRule

TTSRRule represents a stream rule that monitors LLM output for pattern violations and can inject corrective content when triggered.

	type TTSRRule struct {
	    Name      string   `yaml:"name" json:"name"`
	    Scope     string   `yaml:"scope" json:"scope"`         // "text", "thinking", "tool_call", "any"
	    Condition string   `yaml:"condition" json:"condition"` // regex pattern
	    Interrupt bool     `yaml:"interrupt" json:"interrupt"` // true = abort+retry, false = in-band reminder
	    Repeat    string   `yaml:"repeat" json:"repeat"`       // "once" or "after-gap:N"
	    Globs     []string `yaml:"globs" json:"globs"`         // file globs for scoped rules
	    Content   string   `yaml:"-" json:"-"`                 // rule body from markdown content
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

<a name="TacticalScheduler"></a>
## type TacticalScheduler

TacticalScheduler schedules ready steps as queue jobs and handles completion callbacks.

	type TacticalScheduler struct {
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

<a name="NewTacticalScheduler"></a>
### func NewTacticalScheduler

	func NewTacticalScheduler(cfg TacticalSchedulerConfig) *TacticalScheduler

NewTacticalScheduler creates a new tactical scheduler.

<a name="TacticalScheduler.GetJobByID"></a>
### func \(\*TacticalScheduler\) GetJobByID

	func (ts *TacticalScheduler) GetJobByID(ctx context.Context, jobID string) (*queue.Job, error)

GetJobByID retrieves a job from the queue by ID.

<a name="TacticalScheduler.HandleHandoff"></a>
### func \(\*TacticalScheduler\) HandleHandoff

	func (ts *TacticalScheduler) HandleHandoff(ctx context.Context, msg *models.BusMessage) error

HandleHandoff processes a handoff request: creates a new task step for the target agent, optionally rewires downstream dependencies, and publishes a bus event.

<a name="TacticalScheduler.OnJobCompleted"></a>
### func \(\*TacticalScheduler\) OnJobCompleted

	func (ts *TacticalScheduler) OnJobCompleted(ctx context.Context, jobID string, result json.RawMessage) error

OnJobCompleted handles a completed job by updating the step, promoting newly unblocked steps, and checking task completion.

<a name="TacticalScheduler.OnJobFailed"></a>
### func \(\*TacticalScheduler\) OnJobFailed

	func (ts *TacticalScheduler) OnJobFailed(ctx context.Context, jobID, jobErr string) error

OnJobFailed handles a failed job by updating the step and potentially marking the task as failed.

<a name="TacticalScheduler.ScheduleReadySteps"></a>
### func \(\*TacticalScheduler\) ScheduleReadySteps

	func (ts *TacticalScheduler) ScheduleReadySteps(ctx context.Context, taskID string) error

ScheduleReadySteps finds ready steps for a task and enqueues them as jobs. Steps that cannot be scheduled due to semaphore limits remain in "ready" state and will be retried on the next scheduling cycle.

<a name="TacticalSchedulerConfig"></a>
## type TacticalSchedulerConfig

TacticalSchedulerConfig holds configuration for the tactical scheduler.

	type TacticalSchedulerConfig struct {
	    StepStore              *task.StepStore
	    TaskStore              *task.Store
	    Queue                  queue.Queue
	    Registry               *AgentRegistry
	    Bus                    *bus.MessageBus
	    PairManager            *PairManager
	    ReviewManager          *ReviewManager
	    ValidatorManager       *validator.ValidatorManager
	    EscalationManager      *EscalationManager
	    Logger                 *slog.Logger
	    MaxConcurrentJobs      int                // Global concurrent job limit (default: 10)
	    MaxConcurrentPerAgent  int                // Per-agent concurrent job limit (default: 3)
	    ValidationGateInterval int                // Run validation gate every N steps (default: 3, 0 to disable)
	    MaxHandoffSteps        int                // Max handoff steps per task (0 = unlimited, default: 5)
	    HandoffUseAmendment    bool               // Route handoffs through amendment system (default: true)
	    AmendmentManager       AmendmentSubmitter // Optional: enables amendment-based step creation
	}

<a name="TaskMessage"></a>
## type TaskMessage

TaskMessage is the standardized inter\-agent communication format. This follows JSONL format for streaming and logging.

	type TaskMessage struct {
	    // Type identifies the message type.
	    Type MessageType `json:"type"`
	
	    // ID is a unique message identifier.
	    ID  string `json:"id"`
	
	    // TaskID references the parent task (for results/errors).
	    TaskID string `json:"task_id,omitempty"`
	
	    // From is the source agent ID.
	    From string `json:"from"`
	
	    // To is the target agent ID.
	    To  string `json:"to"`
	
	    // Action specifies what action is requested.
	    Action ActionType `json:"action"`
	
	    // Timestamp is when the message was created.
	    Timestamp time.Time `json:"timestamp"`
	
	    // Payload contains action-specific data.
	    Payload json.RawMessage `json:"payload"`
	}

<a name="NewResultMessage"></a>
### func NewResultMessage

	func NewResultMessage(taskID, from string, result *ResultPayload) (*TaskMessage, error)

NewResultMessage creates a result message for a task.

<a name="NewTaskMessage"></a>
### func NewTaskMessage

	func NewTaskMessage(from, to string, action ActionType, payload any) (*TaskMessage, error)

NewTaskMessage creates a new task message.

<a name="ParseTaskMessage"></a>
### func ParseTaskMessage

	func ParseTaskMessage(line string) (*TaskMessage, error)

ParseTaskMessage parses a JSONL line into a TaskMessage.

<a name="TaskMessage.GetErrorPayload"></a>
### func \(\*TaskMessage\) GetErrorPayload

	func (m *TaskMessage) GetErrorPayload() (*ErrorPayload, error)

GetErrorPayload extracts ErrorPayload from a message.

<a name="TaskMessage.GetResultPayload"></a>
### func \(\*TaskMessage\) GetResultPayload

	func (m *TaskMessage) GetResultPayload() (*ResultPayload, error)

GetResultPayload extracts ResultPayload from a message.

<a name="TaskMessage.GetTaskPayload"></a>
### func \(\*TaskMessage\) GetTaskPayload

	func (m *TaskMessage) GetTaskPayload() (*TaskPayload, error)

GetTaskPayload extracts TaskPayload from a message.

<a name="TaskMessage.ToJSONL"></a>
### func \(\*TaskMessage\) ToJSONL

	func (m *TaskMessage) ToJSONL() (string, error)

ToJSONL converts the message to a single JSONL line.

<a name="TaskPayload"></a>
## type TaskPayload

TaskPayload is the payload for "execute" and "delegate" actions.

	type TaskPayload struct {
	    // Description is what needs to be done.
	    Description string `json:"description"`
	
	    // MemoryRefs are explicit memory IDs for context.
	    MemoryRefs []string `json:"memory_refs,omitempty"`
	
	    // ContextQuery is an auto-search query for additional context.
	    ContextQuery string `json:"context_query,omitempty"`
	
	    // InheritedFrom is the parent task ID for subtasks.
	    InheritedFrom string `json:"inherited_from,omitempty"`
	
	    // Priority is the task priority.
	    Priority TaskPriority `json:"priority,omitempty"`
	
	    // ErrorContext provides error details for debugger handoffs.
	    ErrorContext string `json:"error_context,omitempty"`
	
	    // Constraints are agent-specific execution constraints.
	    Constraints map[string]any `json:"constraints,omitempty"`
	
	    // MemvidZone specifies the memory zone for this task.
	    MemvidZone string `json:"memvid_zone,omitempty"`
	}

<a name="TaskPriority"></a>

	type TaskPriority string

<a name="PriorityLow"></a>

	const (
	    PriorityLow    TaskPriority = "low"
	    PriorityNormal TaskPriority = "normal"
	    PriorityHigh   TaskPriority = "high"
	    PriorityUrgent TaskPriority = "urgent"
	)

	    Criteria []StepCriterion `json:"criteria,omitempty"`
	}

<a name="TeamOrchestrator"></a>
## type TeamOrchestrator

TeamOrchestrator manages team lifecycle via bus subscriptions. It subscribes to team.start to initiate teams, runs the ParallelTeamDriver, and publishes results to team.result and errors to team.error.

	type TeamOrchestrator struct {
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

<a name="NewTeamOrchestrator"></a>
### func NewTeamOrchestrator

	func NewTeamOrchestrator(deps TeamOrchestratorDeps) *TeamOrchestrator

<a name="TeamOrchestrator.ActiveTeamCount"></a>
### func \(\*TeamOrchestrator\) ActiveTeamCount

	func (to *TeamOrchestrator) ActiveTeamCount() int

<a name="TeamOrchestrator.AssignSubtask"></a>
### func \(\*TeamOrchestrator\) AssignSubtask

	func (to *TeamOrchestrator) AssignSubtask(ctx context.Context, sessionID string, assignment SubtaskAssignment) error

AssignSubtask assigns a subtask to a specific team member. The assignment is published to the per\-session message topic so the member's running agent loop can pick it up, and the team state is updated.

<a name="TeamOrchestrator.BroadcastMessage"></a>

	func (to *TeamOrchestrator) BroadcastMessage(ctx context.Context, sessionID string, tm TeamBroadcastMessage) error

BroadcastMessage sends a message to team members via the per\-session message topic. If targetAgent is empty, the message is a broadcast.

ReceiveResult records a partial result from a team member and publishes it to the per\-session result topic.

<a name="TeamOrchestrator.Start"></a>
### func \(\*TeamOrchestrator\) Start

	func (to *TeamOrchestrator) Start(ctx context.Context) error

Start subscribes to team.start and begins processing team requests.

<a name="TeamOrchestrator.Status"></a>
### func \(\*TeamOrchestrator\) Status

	func (to *TeamOrchestrator) Status(ctx context.Context, sessionID string) (*TeamSessionState, error)

Status returns the current status of a team session.

<a name="TeamOrchestrator.Stop"></a>
### func \(\*TeamOrchestrator\) Stop

	func (to *TeamOrchestrator) Stop(ctx context.Context) error

Stop gracefully stops the orchestrator.

<a name="TeamOrchestratorDeps"></a>
## type TeamOrchestratorDeps

TeamOrchestratorDeps holds dependencies for creating a TeamOrchestrator.

	type TeamOrchestratorDeps struct {
	    CollabEngine *CollaborationEngine
	    Bus          *bus.MessageBus
	    Logger       *slog.Logger
	}

<a name="TeamPreset"></a>
## type TeamPreset

TeamPreset defines a pre\-configured team template for common multi\-agent workflows.

	type TeamPreset struct {
	    // Name is the unique identifier for this preset (e.g. "hyperplan", "security_research").
	    Name string `json:"name"`
	    // Description is a human-readable description of what this preset does.
	    Description string `json:"description"`
	    // LeadAgent is the agent ID that orchestrates and synthesizes results.
	    LeadAgent string `json:"lead_agent"`
	    // Roster is the list of specialist agent IDs.
	    Roster []string `json:"roster"`
	    // MaxConcurrent limits parallel execution.
	    MaxConcurrent int `json:"max_concurrent"`
	    // PromptTemplate is a Go text/template string that receives the task description
	    // and assigns each roster member their specialist role.
	    PromptTemplate string `json:"prompt_template"`
	}

<a name="GetTeamPreset"></a>
### func GetTeamPreset

	func GetTeamPreset(name string) (*TeamPreset, error)

GetTeamPreset returns a preset by name. Returns an error if the preset is not found.

<a name="ListTeamPresets"></a>
### func ListTeamPresets

	func ListTeamPresets() []TeamPreset

ListTeamPresets returns all available team presets.

<a name="TeamSessionState"></a>
## type TeamSessionState

	type TeamSessionState struct {
	    SessionID     string                       `json:"session_id"`
	    TaskID        string                       `json:"task_id"`
	    LeadAgent     string                       `json:"lead_agent"`
	    Roster        []string                     `json:"roster"`
	    Phase         string                       `json:"phase"` // "running", "synthesizing", "completed", "failed"
	    MemberResults map[string]*TeamMemberResult `json:"member_results,omitempty"`
	    FinalOutput   string                       `json:"final_output,omitempty"`
	    StartTime     time.Time                    `json:"start_time"`
	}

<a name="TeamStartRequest"></a>
## type TeamStartRequest

TeamStartRequest initiates a new team parallel session.

	type TeamStartRequest struct {
	    // SessionID is the unique team session identifier.
	    SessionID string `json:"session_id"`
	    // TaskID is the parent task ID.
	    TaskID string `json:"task_id"`
	    // LeadAgent is the lead agent that synthesizes results.
	    LeadAgent string `json:"lead_agent"`
	    // Roster is the list of specialist agent IDs.
	    Roster []string `json:"roster"`
	    // TaskDescription is the task to be distributed to team members.
	    TaskDescription string `json:"task_description"`
	    // MaxConcurrent limits parallel execution (0 = all at once).
	    MaxConcurrent int `json:"max_concurrent,omitempty"`
	    // MemberTimeout is per-member timeout (0 = default from session config).
	    MemberTimeout time.Duration `json:"member_timeout,omitempty"`
	}

<a name="ToolRegistry"></a>
## type ToolRegistry

ToolRegistry provides access to available tools.

This is the agent\-layer interface for looking up and listing tools. It is intentionally narrower than the production \[tools.Registry\] so that unit tests can substitute [PlaceholderToolRegistry](<#PlaceholderToolRegistry>) without dragging in the full tool registry graph.

Relationship to other interfaces:

- \[tools.Tool\] \-\- interface for a single tool \(Name, Description, Parameters, Execute\)
- agent.ToolRegistry \-\- this interface: a collection of tools with lookup \(Get, List, GetDefinitions\)
- \[tools.Registry\] \-\- production implementation that satisfies agent.ToolRegistry
- \[tools.ToolExecutor\] \-\- interface for executing a tool by name with permission checks

The production \[tools.Registry\] satisfies this interface; see \[tools.Registry.GetDefinitions\] which is an alias for compatibility.

	type ToolRegistry interface {
	    // Get retrieves a tool by name.
	    Get(name string) tools.Tool
	    // List returns all available tools.
	    List() []tools.Tool
	    // GetDefinitions returns tool definitions for the LLM.
	    GetDefinitions() []llm.ToolDefinition
	}

<a name="FilterToolsForSkill"></a>
### func FilterToolsForSkill

	func FilterToolsForSkill(registry ToolRegistry, allowedTools []string) ToolRegistry

FilterToolsForSkill creates a filtered tool registry based on a skill's allowed\-tools. This is used when executing skills that have restricted tool access.

<a name="TrackerSessionState"></a>
## type TrackerSessionState

TrackerSessionState holds state for a single conversation session.

	type TrackerSessionState struct {
	    SessionID      string
	    CreatedAt      time.Time
	    LastActivityAt time.Time
	    IntentHistory  []*Intent
	    TotalRequests  int
	    Metrics        SessionMetrics
	    Persisted      bool // true if session has been persisted to memvid
	}

<a name="ValidationPolicy"></a>
## type ValidationPolicy

ValidationPolicy determines which steps require validation and how.

	type ValidationPolicy struct {
	    // Enabled globally
	    Enabled bool
	
	    // Tool hints that ALWAYS require validation
	    RequireValidation []string
	
	    // Tool hints that NEVER require validation (trusted operations)
	    SkipValidation []string
	
	    // Maximum validation retry loops before escalation
	    MaxValidationLoops int
	
	    // Skip validation for these agents
	    SkipValidationAgents []string
	}

<a name="DefaultValidationPolicy"></a>
### func DefaultValidationPolicy

	func DefaultValidationPolicy() *ValidationPolicy

DefaultValidationPolicy returns sensible defaults for validation policy.

<a name="ValidationPolicy.ExceedsMaxValidationLoops"></a>

	func (p *ValidationPolicy) ExceedsMaxValidationLoops(validationLoops int) bool

ExceedsMaxValidationLoops checks if a step has exceeded validation retry loops.

<a name="ValidationPolicy.NeedsValidation"></a>
### func \(\*ValidationPolicy\) NeedsValidation

	func (p *ValidationPolicy) NeedsValidation(step *task.TaskStep) bool

NeedsValidation determines if a step requires validation based on policy.

	}

<a name="VerbosityLevel"></a>
## type VerbosityLevel

VerbosityLevel controls how much progress detail is surfaced.

	type VerbosityLevel int

<a name="VerbosityQuiet"></a>

	const (
	    // VerbosityQuiet shows only high-level completion events (agent end).
	    VerbosityQuiet VerbosityLevel = iota
	    // VerbosityNormal shows tool results and agent completions.
	    VerbosityNormal
	    // VerbosityVerbose shows everything including tool starts and turn boundaries.
	    VerbosityVerbose
	)

<a name="Watchdog"></a>
## type Watchdog

Watchdog monitors agent workers for stuck conditions and aborts them after timeouts.

	type Watchdog struct {
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

<a name="NewWatchdog"></a>
### func NewWatchdog

	func NewWatchdog(cfg config.WatchdogConfig, logger *slog.Logger) *Watchdog

NewWatchdog creates a new Watchdog monitor with the given configuration.

<a name="Watchdog.ActiveWorkerCount"></a>
### func \(\*Watchdog\) ActiveWorkerCount

	func (w *Watchdog) ActiveWorkerCount() int

ActiveWorkerCount returns the number of currently monitored workers.

<a name="Watchdog.Alerts"></a>
### func \(\*Watchdog\) Alerts

	func (w *Watchdog) Alerts() <-chan WatchdogAlert

Alerts returns a read\-only channel for receiving watchdog alerts.

<a name="Watchdog.CaptureReport"></a>
### func \(\*Watchdog\) CaptureReport

	func (w *Watchdog) CaptureReport(workerID, partialResult string) *ReportCapture

CaptureReport captures the partial state of a worker \(used on abort\). This method is safe to call while holding the write lock \(does not re\-acquire it\). When called from checkWorkers \(which holds w.mu\), use captureReportUnsafe instead.

<a name="Watchdog.GetWorkerState"></a>
### func \(\*Watchdog\) GetWorkerState

	func (w *Watchdog) GetWorkerState(workerID string) (*WorkerState, bool)

GetWorkerState returns the current state of a worker \(for inspection\).

<a name="Watchdog.RegisterWorker"></a>
### func \(\*Watchdog\) RegisterWorker

	func (w *Watchdog) RegisterWorker(workerID, taskID, stepID string, cancelFunc context.CancelFunc)

RegisterWorker registers a worker for monitoring. The cancel function is used to abort the worker if it becomes stuck.

<a name="Watchdog.Reports"></a>
### func \(\*Watchdog\) Reports

	func (w *Watchdog) Reports() <-chan ReportCapture

Reports returns a read\-only channel for receiving report captures.

<a name="Watchdog.Start"></a>
### func \(\*Watchdog\) Start

	func (w *Watchdog) Start(ctx context.Context)

Start begins the background monitoring goroutine.

<a name="Watchdog.Stop"></a>
### func \(\*Watchdog\) Stop

	func (w *Watchdog) Stop()

Stop gracefully stops the watchdog monitor.

<a name="Watchdog.UnregisterWorker"></a>
### func \(\*Watchdog\) UnregisterWorker

	func (w *Watchdog) UnregisterWorker(workerID string)

UnregisterWorker removes a worker from monitoring. Should be called when a worker completes or is cleaned up.

<a name="Watchdog.UpdateHeartbeat"></a>
### func \(\*Watchdog\) UpdateHeartbeat

	func (w *Watchdog) UpdateHeartbeat(workerID string, iteration int, stage WorkerStage)

UpdateHeartbeat updates the heartbeat for a worker, indicating it is still alive. Also updates the iteration count and stage.

<a name="WatchdogAlert"></a>
## type WatchdogAlert

WatchdogAlert represents a watchdog alert for a stuck or timed\-out worker.

	type WatchdogAlert struct {
	    Type      WatchdogAlertType
	    WorkerID  string
	    TaskID    string
	    StepID    string
	    Message   string
	    Duration  time.Duration
	    Iteration int
	}

<a name="WatchdogAlertType"></a>
## type WatchdogAlertType

WatchdogAlertType identifies the kind of watchdog alert.

	type WatchdogAlertType string

<a name="AlertTimeout"></a>

	const (
	    AlertTimeout   WatchdogAlertType = "timeout"
	    AlertMaxIter   WatchdogAlertType = "max_iterations"
	    AlertStuck     WatchdogAlertType = "stuck"
	    AlertHeartbeat WatchdogAlertType = "heartbeat_missed"
	)

<a name="Worker"></a>
## type Worker

Worker represents an active agent processing a request.

	type Worker struct {
	    ID             string    `json:"id"`
	    ConversationID string    `json:"conversation_id"`
	    RequestID      string    `json:"request_id"`
	    State          string    `json:"state"` // "processing", "executing_tool", "completed", "error"
	    StartTime      time.Time `json:"start_time"`
	    LastActivity   time.Time `json:"last_activity"`
	    CurrentTool    string    `json:"current_tool,omitempty"`
	}

<a name="WorkerStage"></a>

WorkerStage represents the current stage of a monitored worker.

	type WorkerStage string

<a name="StageThinking"></a>

	const (
	    StageThinking   WorkerStage = "thinking"
	    StageExecuting  WorkerStage = "executing"
	    StageValidating WorkerStage = "validating"
	    StageReviewing  WorkerStage = "reviewing"
	)

<a name="WorkerState"></a>
## type WorkerState

WorkerState tracks the state of a single monitored worker/agent.

	type WorkerState struct {
	    WorkerID      string
	    TaskID        string
	    StepID        string
	    StartTime     time.Time
	    LastHeartbeat time.Time
	    Iteration     int
	    Stage         WorkerStage
	    IsStuck       bool
	    CancelFunc    context.CancelFunc
	}

	    AutoCommit bool   // Automatically commit after writes (default: true)

<a name="WorkspaceManager"></a>
## type WorkspaceManager

WorkspaceManager manages per\-task workspace directories with git tracking. It creates isolated directories under a configurable base path \(default \~/.meept/workspaces/\), initialises each as a git repository, and provides methods for committing plans, reviews, and artifacts at every lifecycle stage.

	type WorkspaceManager struct {
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

<a name="NewWorkspaceManager"></a>

Cleanup removes the workspace directory entirely.

### func \(\*WorkspaceManager\) CreateCheckpoint

	func (w *WorkspaceManager) CreateCheckpoint(ctx context.Context, taskID, label string) (*Checkpoint, error)

CreateCheckpoint creates a checkpoint in the workspace using git tags. The tag format is: checkpoint\-\{taskID\}\-\{label\}\-\{timestamp\}

### func \(\*WorkspaceManager\) DeleteCheckpoint

	func (w *WorkspaceManager) DeleteCheckpoint(ctx context.Context, taskID, label string) error

	func (w *WorkspaceManager) ListCheckpoints(ctx context.Context, taskID string) ([]Checkpoint, error)

ListCheckpoints returns all checkpoints for a task. AGENT\-17 FIX: Tag parsing extracts timestamp from the end \(last numeric segment after the final dash\) and joins everything between taskID and timestamp as the label, correctly handling labels containing dashes \(e.g. "fix\-a\-bug\-1712345678"\).

<a name="MessageBus"></a>
## type MessageBus

MessageBus implements a channel\-based publish/subscribe message bus.

	type MessageBus struct {
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

<a name="New"></a>
### func New

	func New(cfg *Config, logger *slog.Logger) *MessageBus

New creates a new MessageBus.

<a name="MessageBus.Close"></a>
### func \(\*MessageBus\) Close

	func (b *MessageBus) Close()

Close shuts down the message bus and closes all subscriber channels.

<a name="MessageBus.Publish"></a>
### func \(\*MessageBus\) Publish

	func (b *MessageBus) Publish(topic string, msg *models.BusMessage) int

Publish sends a message to all subscribers of the topic. It also publishes to wildcard subscribers \(e.g., "agent.\*" matches "agent.status"\).

<a name="MessageBus.Request"></a>
### func \(\*MessageBus\) Request

	func (b *MessageBus) Request(ctx context.Context, topic string, msg *models.BusMessage) (*models.BusMessage, error)

Request sends a message and waits for a reply.

<a name="MessageBus.Stats"></a>
### func \(\*MessageBus\) Stats

	func (b *MessageBus) Stats() map[string]int

Stats returns current bus statistics.

<a name="MessageBus.Subscribe"></a>
### func \(\*MessageBus\) Subscribe

	func (b *MessageBus) Subscribe(id, topic string) *Subscriber

Subscribe creates a subscription to a topic. The topic can contain wildcards: "agent.\*" matches "agent.status", "agent.error".

<a name="MessageBus.Unsubscribe"></a>
### func \(\*MessageBus\) Unsubscribe

	func (b *MessageBus) Unsubscribe(sub *Subscriber)

Unsubscribe removes a subscription.

<a name="MessageCallback"></a>
## type MessageCallback

MessageCallback is invoked when a message arrives on a subscribed topic.

	type MessageCallback func(ctx context.Context, topic string, msg any)

<a name="Subscriber"></a>
## type Subscriber

Subscriber represents a channel that receives messages.

	type Subscriber struct {
	    ID      string
	    Topic   string
	    Channel chan *models.BusMessage
	}

<a name="SubscriptionHandler"></a>
## type SubscriptionHandler

SubscriptionHandler manages bus subscriptions with automatic lifecycle management. It handles subscribe/dispatch/teardown to eliminate duplicated handler boilerplate.

CORE\-6 FIX: Track all subscribers so Stop\(\) can unsubscribe from each one instead of relying only on per\-goroutine defer. Without this list, if the context is cancelled but a goroutine never reaches its defer \(e.g., due to channel read blocking\), the subscription leaks.

	type SubscriptionHandler struct {
	    // contains filtered or unexported fields
	}

<a name="NewSubscriptionHandler"></a>
### func NewSubscriptionHandler

	func NewSubscriptionHandler(bus *MessageBus, logger *slog.Logger) *SubscriptionHandler

NewSubscriptionHandler creates a new handler

<a name="SubscriptionHandler.Start"></a>
### func \(\*SubscriptionHandler\) Start

	func (h *SubscriptionHandler) Start(parentCtx context.Context)

Start begins listening to all subscribed topics

<a name="SubscriptionHandler.Stop"></a>
### func \(\*SubscriptionHandler\) Stop

	func (h *SubscriptionHandler) Stop()

Stop gracefully shuts down all subscription goroutines

<a name="SubscriptionHandler.Subscribe"></a>
### func \(\*SubscriptionHandler\) Subscribe

	func (h *SubscriptionHandler) Subscribe(topic string, callback MessageCallback)

Subscribe adds a topic→callback mapping

## func EnsureDataDir

	func EnsureDataDir(cfg *Config) error

EnsureDataDir creates the data directory if it doesn't exist.

<a name="ExpandEnvVars"></a>
## func ExpandEnvVars

	func ExpandEnvVars(s string) string

ExpandEnvVars expands environment variables in a string. Uses a regex rather than os.ExpandEnv because configs use both $VAR and $\{VAR\} syntax \(os.ExpandEnv only supports the former\).

<a name="FilterEnabledAgents"></a>
## func FilterEnabledAgents

	func FilterEnabledAgents(agents map[string]*AgentDefinition) map[string]*AgentDefinition

FilterEnabledAgents returns only enabled agent definitions.

<a name="LoadAgentDefinitions"></a>
## func LoadAgentDefinitions

	func LoadAgentDefinitions(configDirs []string) (map[string]*AgentDefinition, error)

LoadAgentDefinitions loads all agent definitions from the configured directories. Later directories in the list override earlier ones \(for the same agent ID\).

<a name="LoadAgentDefinitionsDefault"></a>
## func LoadAgentDefinitionsDefault

	func LoadAgentDefinitionsDefault(cfg *AgentsConfig) (map[string]*AgentDefinition, error)

LoadAgentDefinitionsDefault loads agents from default locations. Prefers JSON5 single file, falls back to TOML directory format.

<a name="LoadAgentDefinitionsDefaultWithJSON5"></a>
## func LoadAgentDefinitionsDefaultWithJSON5

	func LoadAgentDefinitionsDefaultWithJSON5(cfg *AgentsConfig) (map[string]*AgentDefinition, error)

LoadAgentDefinitionsDefaultWithJSON5 tries JSON5 first, then TOML.

## func LoadAgentDefinitionsJSON5

	func LoadAgentDefinitionsJSON5(path string) (map[string]*AgentDefinition, error)

LoadAgentDefinitionsJSON5 loads all agent definitions from a JSON5 file.

<a name="LoadJSON5"></a>
## func LoadJSON5

	func LoadJSON5(path string, v any) error

LoadJSON5 reads a JSON5 file, expands environment variables, standardizes to JSON, and unmarshals into v.

<a name="LoadJSON5WithDefault"></a>
## func LoadJSON5WithDefault

	func LoadJSON5WithDefault(path string, v any) error

LoadJSON5WithDefault loads JSON5 from path, or returns default if not found.

<a name="MergeAgentDefaults"></a>
## func MergeAgentDefaults

	func MergeAgentDefaults(agent *AgentDefinition)

MergeAgentDefaults applies default values to an agent definition.

<a name="ParseLogLevel"></a>
## func ParseLogLevel

	func ParseLogLevel(level string) slog.Level

ParseLogLevel converts a string log level to slog.Level.

<a name="StripJSON5Comments"></a>
## func StripJSON5Comments

	func StripJSON5Comments(s string) string

StripJSON5Comments converts JSON5 to strict JSON, handling comments, trailing commas, and unquoted keys. It delegates to hujson.Standardize for full JSON5 spec compliance.

<a name="UnmarshalJSON5"></a>
## func UnmarshalJSON5

	func UnmarshalJSON5(data []byte, v any) error

UnmarshalJSON5 parses JSON5\-formatted bytes into a struct. Unlike LoadJSON5, this does NOT expand environment variables. It also handles Go\-style duration literals \(e.g. 30s, 2m\) and Go duration string values \(e.g. "30s"\) in JSON.

<a name="ValidateAgentDefinition"></a>
## func ValidateAgentDefinition

	func ValidateAgentDefinition(agent *AgentDefinition) error

ValidateAgentDefinition validates an agent definition.

<a name="AIInfraConfig"></a>
## type AIInfraConfig

AIInfraConfig holds AI infrastructure settings for self\-improvement.

	type AIInfraConfig struct {
	    Enabled         bool    `json:"enabled"          toml:"enabled"`
	    BaseURL         string  `json:"base_url"         toml:"base_url"`
	    APIKeyEnv       string  `json:"api_key_env"      toml:"api_key_env"`
	    AnalysisModel   string  `json:"analysis_model"   toml:"analysis_model"`
	    GenerationModel string  `json:"generation_model" toml:"generation_model"`
	    ReviewModel     string  `json:"review_model"     toml:"review_model"`
	    TimeoutSeconds  float64 `json:"timeout_seconds"  toml:"timeout_seconds"`
	    MaxRetries      int     `json:"max_retries"      toml:"max_retries"`
	}

<a name="ASTConfig"></a>
## type ASTConfig

ASTConfig holds AST parsing settings.

	type ASTConfig struct {
	    // CacheEnabled enables parse result caching
	    CacheEnabled bool `json:"cache_enabled" toml:"cache_enabled"`
	    // CacheMaxSize is the maximum number of cached parse results
	    CacheMaxSize int `json:"cache_max_size" toml:"cache_max_size"`
	    // CacheTTLMinutes is how long cached results remain valid
	    CacheTTLMinutes int `json:"cache_ttl_minutes" toml:"cache_ttl_minutes"`
	}

<a name="AgentCompactionConfig"></a>
## type AgentCompactionConfig

AgentCompactionConfig holds context compaction settings for the agent.

	type AgentCompactionConfig struct {
	    Enabled           bool   `json:"enabled"            toml:"enabled"`
	    ReserveTokens     int    `json:"reserve_tokens"      toml:"reserve_tokens"`
	    KeepRecentTokens  int    `json:"keep_recent_tokens"  toml:"keep_recent_tokens"`
	    MaxResponseTokens int    `json:"max_response_tokens" toml:"max_response_tokens"`
	    SummaryFormat     string `json:"summary_format"      toml:"summary_format"`
	    TrackFileOps      bool   `json:"track_file_ops"       toml:"track_file_ops"`
	    TimeoutSeconds    int    `json:"timeout_seconds"      toml:"timeout_seconds"`
	}

<a name="AgentConfig"></a>
## type AgentConfig

AgentConfig holds agent loop settings.

	type AgentConfig struct {
	    // ProgressEnabled turns on streaming progress updates
	    ProgressEnabled bool `json:"progress_enabled" toml:"progress_enabled"`
	    // ProgressIntervalSeconds is the minimum interval between progress events
	    ProgressIntervalSeconds int `json:"progress_interval_seconds" toml:"progress_interval_seconds"`
	    // Cache holds tool result caching settings
	    Cache CacheConfig `json:"cache" toml:"cache"`
	    // Errors holds error handling settings
	    Errors ErrorsConfig `json:"errors" toml:"errors"`
	    // Review holds code review settings
	    Review ReviewConfig `json:"review" toml:"review"`
	    // Validation holds task completion validation settings
	    Validation ValidationConfig `json:"validation" toml:"validation"`
	    // Watchdog holds agent monitoring settings
	    Watchdog WatchdogConfig `json:"watchdog" toml:"watchdog"`
	    // Queues holds steering and follow-up message queue settings
	    Queues AgentQueuesConfig `json:"queues" toml:"queues"`
	    // Compaction holds context compaction settings
	    Compaction AgentCompactionConfig `json:"compaction" toml:"compaction"`
	}

<a name="AgentConstraintsConfig"></a>
## type AgentConstraintsConfig

AgentConstraintsConfig holds agent operational constraints.

	type AgentConstraintsConfig struct {
	    MaxIterations    int `json:"max_iterations"      toml:"max_iterations"`
	    TimeoutSeconds   int `json:"timeout_seconds"     toml:"timeout_seconds"`
	    MaxTokensPerTurn int `json:"max_tokens_per_turn" toml:"max_tokens_per_turn"`
	    MaxMemoryRefs    int `json:"max_memory_refs"     toml:"max_memory_refs"`
	}

<a name="AgentConstraintsConfig.ToTimeout"></a>
### func \(AgentConstraintsConfig\) ToTimeout

	func (c AgentConstraintsConfig) ToTimeout() time.Duration

ToTimeout converts TimeoutSeconds to time.Duration.

<a name="AgentDefinition"></a>
## type AgentDefinition

AgentDefinition represents an agent definition from a TOML or JSON5 file.

	type AgentDefinition struct {
	    ID          string `json:"id"           toml:"id"`
	    Name        string `json:"name"         toml:"name"`
	    Role        string `json:"role"         toml:"role"` // "dispatcher", "executor", "conversational", "reviewer"
	    Description string `json:"description"  toml:"description"`
	    Model       string `json:"model"        toml:"model"`
	    Enabled     bool   `json:"enabled"      toml:"enabled"`
	    CanDelegate bool   `json:"can_delegate" toml:"can_delegate"`
	
	    // Tools and capabilities
	    AdditionalTools []string `json:"additional_tools" toml:"additional_tools"`
	    Capabilities    []string `json:"capabilities"     toml:"capabilities"`
	
	    // Prompt composition
	    PromptComponents []string `json:"prompt_components" toml:"prompt_components"`
	
	    // Constraints
	    Constraints AgentConstraintsConfig `json:"constraints" toml:"constraints"`
	}

<a name="GetAgentsByRole"></a>
### func GetAgentsByRole

	func GetAgentsByRole(agents map[string]*AgentDefinition, role string) []*AgentDefinition

GetAgentsByRole returns agents with a specific role.

<a name="AgentDefinitionJSON5"></a>
## type AgentDefinitionJSON5

AgentDefinitionJSON5 represents an agent in the new JSON5 format.

	type AgentDefinitionJSON5 struct {
	    ID               string                 `json:"id"`
	    Name             string                 `json:"name"`
	    Role             string                 `json:"role"`
	    Description      string                 `json:"description"`
	    Model            string                 `json:"model"`
	    Enabled          bool                   `json:"enabled"`
	    CanDelegate      bool                   `json:"can_delegate"`
	    AdditionalTools  []string               `json:"additional_tools"`
	    Capabilities     []string               `json:"capabilities"`
	    PromptComponents []string               `json:"prompt_components"`
	    Constraints      AgentConstraintsConfig `json:"constraints"`
	}

<a name="AgentQueuesConfig"></a>
## type AgentQueuesConfig

AgentQueuesConfig holds steering and follow\-up message queue settings.

	type AgentQueuesConfig struct {
	    // Enabled controls whether the steering/follow-up queue feature is active.
	    // When false, agent loops are created without message queues and all
	    // messages go through the standard synchronous path.
	    Enabled bool `json:"enabled" toml:"enabled"`
	    // SteeringDrain controls how the steering queue drains.
	    // Always "one" -- only one steering message is valid at a time.
	    SteeringDrain string `json:"steering_drain" toml:"steering_drain"`
	    // FollowUpDrain controls how the follow-up queue drains.
	    // "one" returns a single message, "all" returns all pending.
	    FollowUpDrain string `json:"followup_drain" toml:"followup_drain"`
	    // MaxSteering is the maximum number of steering messages allowed.
	    // Low value -- they're urgent and should interrupt immediately.
	    MaxSteering int `json:"max_steering" toml:"max_steering"`
	    // MaxFollowUp is the maximum number of follow-up messages allowed.
	    MaxFollowUp int `json:"max_followup" toml:"max_followup"`
	    // PersistFollowUp enables hybrid persistence (write-behind to SQLite).
	    PersistFollowUp bool `json:"persist_followup" toml:"persist_followup"`
	    // FlushDelayMs is the write-behind flush delay in milliseconds.
	    // When PersistFollowUp is true, messages are batched in memory and
	    // flushed to disk after this delay or on daemon shutdown.
	    FlushDelayMs int `json:"flush_delay_ms" toml:"flush_delay_ms"`
	}

<a name="AgentsConfig"></a>
## type AgentsConfig

AgentsConfig holds agent configuration settings.

	type AgentsConfig struct {
	    // Enabled enables the multi-agent system with TOML-based agent definitions.
	    Enabled bool `json:"enabled" toml:"enabled"`
	
	    // ConfigDirs are directories to search for agent definition TOML files.
	    // Searched in order; later directories override earlier ones.
	    ConfigDirs []string `json:"config_dirs" toml:"config_dirs"`
	
	    // PromptsDir is the base directory for prompt components.
	    PromptsDir string `json:"prompts_dir" toml:"prompts_dir"`
	
	    // DefaultModel is the fallback model for agents that don't specify one.
	    DefaultModel string `json:"default_model" toml:"default_model"`
	
	    // DispatcherID is the agent ID that handles intake/routing.
	    DispatcherID string `json:"dispatcher_id" toml:"dispatcher_id"`
	}

<a name="AgentsFileJSON5"></a>
## type AgentsFileJSON5

AgentsFileJSON5 is the root of the agents.json5 file.

	type AgentsFileJSON5 struct {
	    Agents []AgentDefinitionJSON5 `json:"agents"`
	}

	    AutoPauseOnConsecutiveFails int    `json:"auto_pause_on_consecutive_failures" toml:"auto_pause_on_consecutive_failures"`
	    WebhookEnabled              bool   `json:"webhook_enabled" toml:"webhook_enabled"`
	}

	type BudgetConfig struct {
	    HourlyTokenLimit     int     `json:"hourly_token_limit"  toml:"hourly_token_limit"`
	    DailyTokenLimit      int     `json:"daily_token_limit"   toml:"daily_token_limit"`
	    DailyCostLimit       float64 `json:"daily_cost_limit"    toml:"daily_cost_limit"`
	    HourlyCostLimit      float64 `json:"hourly_cost_limit"   toml:"hourly_cost_limit"`
	    RateLimitRPM         int     `json:"rate_limit_rpm"      toml:"rate_limit_rpm"`
	    Aggressiveness       float64 `json:"aggressiveness"      toml:"aggressiveness"`
	    PerTaskTokenLimit    int     `json:"per_task_token_limit" toml:"per_task_token_limit"`
